    36→const rpcHandler = new RPCHandler(router);
    37→
    38→const ALLOWED_ORIGIN = process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGIN ?? "http://localhost:4011";
    39→const STATIC_DIR = join(
    40→	import.meta.dir,
    41→	"..",
    42→	process.env.STATIC_DIR ?? "public",
    43→);
    44→
    45→function corsHeaders(origin: string | null): Record<string, string> {
    46→	if (origin !== ALLOWED_ORIGIN) return {};
    47→	return {
    48→		"Access-Control-Allow-Origin": ALLOWED_ORIGIN,
    49→		"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials": "true",
    50→		"Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS",
    51→		"Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization",
    52→	};
    53→}
    54→
    55→const port = process.env.PORT ?? 4010;
    56→
    57→const server = Bun.serve({
    58→	port,
    59→	async fetch(request) {
    60→		const origin = request.headers.get("Origin");
    61→		const cors = corsHeaders(origin);
    62→
    63→		if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
    64→			return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: cors });
    65→		}
    66→
    67→		const url = new URL(request.url);
    68→
    69→		// Health check for Fly.io (must be GET-accessible)
    70→		if (url.pathname === "/health") {
    71→			return Response.json({ status: "ok", timestamp: Date.now() });
    72→		}
    73→
    74→		if (url.pathname === "/api/cli-token") {
    75→			const session = await auth.api.getSession({
    76→				headers: request.headers,
    77→			});
    78→			if (!session) {
    79→				return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not authenticated" }), {
    80→					status: 401,
    81→					headers: { ...cors, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    82→				});
    83→			}
    84→			return new Response(JSON.stringify({ token: session.session.token }), {
    85→				status: 200,
    86→				headers: { ...cors, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    87→			});
    88→		}
    89→
    90→		if (url.pathname.startsWith("/api/auth")) {
    91→			const response = await auth.handler(request);
    92→			for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(cors)) {
    93→				response.headers.set(key, value);
    94→			}
    95→			return response;
    96→		}
    97→
    98→		const { matched, response } = await rpcHandler.handle(request, {
    99→			prefix: "/rpc",
   100→			context: await getContext(request),
   101→		});
   102→
   103→		if (matched) {
   104→			// Log 500 errors for debugging
   105→			if (response.status >= 500) {
   106→				const body = await response.clone().text();
   107→				console.error(`[RPC ${url.pathname}] ${response.status}:`, body);
   108→			}
   109→			for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(cors)) {
   110→				response.headers.set(key, value);
   111→			}
   112→			return response;
   113→		}
   114→
   115→		// Static file serving (production: frontend assets)
   116→		const filePath = join(STATIC_DIR, url.pathname);
   117→		const file = Bun.file(filePath);
   118→		if (await file.exists()) {
   119→			return new Response(file);
   120→		}
   121→
   122→		// SPA fallback: serve index.html for non-API routes
   123→		const indexFile = Bun.file(join(STATIC_DIR, "index.html"));
   124→		if (await indexFile.exists()) {
   125→			return new Response(indexFile);
   126→		}
   127→
   128→		return new Response("Not found", { status: 404, headers: cors });
   129→	},
   130→});
   131→
   132→async function getContext(request: Request) {
   133→	const session = await auth.api.getSession({
   134→		headers: request.headers,
   135→	});
   136→	return {
   137→		user: session?.user ?? null,
   138→		session: session?.session ?? null,
   139→	};
   140→}
   141→
   142→console.log(`API server listening on http://localhost:${server.port}`);
   143→"}]},"uuid":"00b7982e-1017-4cc7-9dee-70024f70c674","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:58.254Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"1a3457d8-93f4-4b45-8b85-7383479a70d1"}

{"parentUuid":"3f488925-d502-4146-a483-2bf2d68efd8a","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FAybLBEB9Kvo9bbm3yr2Ex","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type {

     7→import {

    13→// Pricing constants: input=$3/M tokens, output=$15/M tokens
    14→const INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION = 3.0;
    15→const OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION = 15.0;
    16→const DEFAULT_DEV_HOURLY_RATE = 100;
    17→
    18→// ROI calculation constants
    19→const CODE_PERCENTAGE = 0.65; // 65% of output tokens are actual code
    20→const TOKENS_PER_LOC = 15; // Average tokens per line of code
    21→const LOC_PER_HOUR = 30; // Developer baseline: 30 lines per hour without Claude
    22→

    78→/**
    79→ * Get comprehensive ROI metrics with period-over-period comparison
    80→ */
    81→export async function getROIMetrics(
    82→	orgId: string,
    83→	days = 7,
    84→): Promise<ROIMetrics> {
    85→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    86→	const d = Number(days);
    87→
    88→	const query = `
    89→    WITH current_period AS (
    90→      SELECT
    91→        COUNT(*) as total_sessions,
    92→        SUM(input_tokens) as total_input_tokens,
    93→        SUM(output_tokens) as total_output_tokens,
    94→        SUM(total_tokens) as total_tokens,
    95→        SUM(actual_duration_min) / 60.0 as total_hours,
    96→        AVG(success_score) as avg_success_score,
    97→        COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as active_developers,
    98→        SUM(has_commit) as total_commits,
    99→        now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY as period_start,
   100→        now64(3) as period_end
   101→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   102→      WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   103→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   104→    ),
   105→    previous_period AS (
   106→      SELECT
   107→        COUNT(*) as total_sessions,
   108→        SUM(input_tokens) as total_input_tokens,
   109→        SUM(output_tokens) as total_output_tokens,
   110→        SUM(has_commit) as total_commits,
   111→        now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d * 2} DAY as period_start,
   112→        now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY as period_end
   113→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   114→      WHERE session_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d * 2} DAY
   115→        AND session_date < now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY
   116→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   117→    )
   118→    SELECT
   119→      c.total_sessions,
   120→      c.total_input_tokens,
   121→      c.total_output_tokens,
   122→      c.total_tokens,
   123→      c.total_hours,
   124→      c.avg_success_score,
   125→      c.active_developers,
   126→      c.total_commits,
   127→      round((c.total_output_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   128→            (c.total_input_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 2) as total_cost,
   129→      round((c.total_output_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   130→            (c.total_input_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 4) / c.total_sessions as cost_per_session,
   131→      if(c.total_commits > 0,
   132→        round((c.total_output_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   133→              (c.total_input_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 4) / c.total_commits,
   134→        0) as cost_per_commit,
   135→      round((p.total_output_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   136→            (p.total_input_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 2) as prev_total_cost,
   137→      round((p.total_output_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   138→            (p.total_input_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 4) / p.total_sessions as prev_cost_per_session,
   139→      if(p.total_commits > 0,
   140→        round((p.total_output_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   141→              (p.total_input_tokens / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 4) / p.total_commits,
   142→        0) as prev_cost_per_commit,
   143→      p.total_commits as prev_total_commits,
   144→      p.total_output_tokens as prev_total_output_tokens,
   145→      formatDateTime(c.period_start, '%Y-%m-%d') as current_period_start,
   146→      formatDateTime(c.period_end, '%Y-%m-%d') as current_period_end,
   147→      formatDateTime(p.period_start, '%Y-%m-%d') as previous_period_start,
   148→      formatDateTime(p.period_end, '%Y-%m-%d') as previous_period_end
   149→    FROM current_period c
   150→    CROSS JOIN previous_period p
   151→  `;
   152→
   153→	const result = await queryClickhouse<ROIMetricsQueryResult>(query);
   154→
   155→	if (!result || result.length === 0) {
   156→		throw new Error("No data available for ROI metrics");
   157→	}
   158→
   159→	const data = result[0] as ROIMetricsQueryResult;
   160→
   161→	// Calculate percentage changes
   162→	const totalCostChangePct =
   163→		data.prev_total_cost > 0
   164→			? ((data.total_cost - data.prev_total_cost) / data.prev_total_cost) * 100
   165→			: 0;
   166→
   167→	const costPerSessionChangePct =
   168→		data.prev_cost_per_session > 0
   169→			? ((data.cost_per_session - data.prev_cost_per_session) /
   170→					data.prev_cost_per_session) *
   171→				100
   172→			: 0;
   173→
   174→	const costPerCommitChangePct =
   175→		data.prev_cost_per_commit > 0 && data.cost_per_commit > 0
   176→			? ((data.cost_per_commit - data.prev_cost_per_commit) /
   177→					data.prev_cost_per_commit) *
   178→				100
   179→			: 0;
   180→
   181→	// Calculate productivity improvement
   182→	const currentCommitsPerDollar =
   183→		data.total_cost > 0 ? data.total_commits / data.total_cost : 0;
   184→	const prevCommitsPerDollar =
   185→		data.prev_total_cost > 0
   186→			? data.prev_total_commits / data.prev_total_cost
   187→			: 0;
   188→	const productivityImprovementPct =
   189→		prevCommitsPerDollar > 0
   190→			? ((currentCommitsPerDollar - prevCommitsPerDollar) /
   191→					prevCommitsPerDollar) *
   192→				100
   193→			: 0;
   194→
   195→	// Token utilization rate (compared to baseline of 10M tokens per week)
   196→	const baselineTokensPerWeek = 10_000_000;
   197→	const tokenUtilizationRate =
   198→		(data.total_tokens / baselineTokensPerWeek) * 100;
   199→
   200→	// Developer hours saved
   201→	const currentLOC =
   202→		(data.total_output_tokens * CODE_PERCENTAGE) / TOKENS_PER_LOC;
   203→	const previousLOC =
   204→		(data.prev_total_output_tokens * CODE_PERCENTAGE) / TOKENS_PER_LOC;
   205→
   206→	const currentHoursSaved = currentLOC / LOC_PER_HOUR;
   207→	const previousHoursSaved = previousLOC / LOC_PER_HOUR;
   208→
   209→	const currentValueCreated = currentHoursSaved * DEFAULT_DEV_HOURLY_RATE;
   210→	const currentDollarValueSaved = currentValueCreated - data.total_cost;
   211→
   212→	const roiPercentage =
   213→		data.total_cost > 0 ? (currentDollarValueSaved / data.total_cost) * 100 : 0;
   214→
   215→	const devHoursSavedChangePct =
   216→		previousHoursSaved > 0
   217→			? ((currentHoursSaved - previousHoursSaved) / previousHoursSaved) * 100
   218→			: 0;
   219→
   220→	return {
   221→		total_cost: Number(data.total_cost) || 0,
   222→		total_cost_change_pct: Number(totalCostChangePct.toFixed(2)),
   223→		cost_per_session: Number(data.cost_per_session) || 0,
   224→		cost_per_session_change_pct: Number(costPerSessionChangePct.toFixed(2)),
   225→		cost_per_commit: Number(data.cost_per_commit) || 0,
   226→		cost_per_commit_change_pct: Number(costPerCommitChangePct.toFixed(2)),
   227→		total_tokens: Number(data.total_tokens) || 0,
   228→		input_tokens: Number(data.total_input_tokens) || 0,
   229→		output_tokens: Number(data.total_output_tokens) || 0,
   230→		token_utilization_rate: Number(tokenUtilizationRate.toFixed(2)),
   231→		total_sessions: Number(data.total_sessions) || 0,
   232→		total_commits: Number(data.total_commits) || 0,
   233→		total_hours: Number(data.total_hours) || 0,
   234→		active_developers: Number(data.active_developers) || 0,
   235→		avg_success_score: Number(data.avg_success_score) || 0,
   236→		commits_per_dollar: parseFloat(currentCommitsPerDollar.toFixed(2)),
   237→		sessions_per_dollar:
   238→			data.total_cost > 0
   239→				? parseFloat((data.total_sessions / data.total_cost).toFixed(2))
   240→				: 0,
   241→		productivity_improvement_pct: parseFloat(
   242→			productivityImprovementPct.toFixed(2),
   243→		),
   244→		estimated_loc_generated: parseFloat(currentLOC.toFixed(0)),
   245→		dev_hours_saved: parseFloat(currentHoursSaved.toFixed(2)),
   246→		dev_hours_saved_change_pct: parseFloat(devHoursSavedChangePct.toFixed(2)),
   247→		dollar_value_saved: parseFloat(currentDollarValueSaved.toFixed(2)),
   248→		roi_percentage: parseFloat(roiPercentage.toFixed(2)),
   249→		current_period_start: data.current_period_start,
   250→		current_period_end: data.current_period_end,
   251→		previous_period_start: data.previous_period_start,
   252→		previous_period_end: data.previous_period_end,
   253→	};
   254→}

   256→/**
   257→ * Get weekly ROI trends for charting
   258→ */
   259→export async function getROITrends(
   260→	orgId: string,
   261→	days = 56,
   262→): Promise<ROITrend[]> {
   263→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   264→	const d = Number(days);
   265→
   266→	const query = `
   267→    SELECT
   268→      toMonday(session_date) as week_start,
   269→      COUNT(*) as total_sessions,
   270→      SUM(input_tokens) as total_input_tokens,
   271→      SUM(output_tokens) as total_output_tokens,
   272→      SUM(total_tokens) as total_tokens,
   273→      SUM(has_commit) as total_commits,
   274→      COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as active_developers,
   275→      AVG(success_score) as avg_success_score,
   276→      round((SUM(output_tokens) / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   277→            (SUM(input_tokens) / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 2) as total_cost
   278→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   279→    WHERE session_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY
   280→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   281→    GROUP BY week_start
   282→    ORDER BY week_start ASC
   283→  `;
   284→
   285→	const result = await queryClickhouse<TrendQueryResult>(query);
   286→
   287→	return result.map((row) => {
   288→		const productivityScore =
   289→			row.total_cost > 0 ? (row.total_commits / row.total_cost) * 100 : 0;
   290→
   291→		return {
   292→			week_start: row.week_start,
   293→			total_cost: Number(row.total_cost) || 0,
   294→			total_sessions: Number(row.total_sessions) || 0,
   295→			total_commits: Number(row.total_commits) || 0,
   296→			active_developers: Number(row.active_developers) || 0,
   297→			avg_success_score: Number(row.avg_success_score) || 0,
   298→			total_tokens: Number(row.total_tokens) || 0,
   299→			output_tokens: Number(row.total_output_tokens) || 0,
   300→			productivity_score: parseFloat(productivityScore.toFixed(2)),
   301→		};
   302→	});
   303→}

   305→/**
   306→ * Get cost breakdown by developer
   307→ */
   308→export async function getDeveloperCostBreakdown(
   309→	orgId: string,
   310→	days = 30,
   311→): Promise<DeveloperCostBreakdown[]> {
   312→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   313→	const d = Number(days);
   314→
   315→	const query = `
   316→    SELECT
   317→      user_id,
   318→      COUNT(*) as total_sessions,
   319→      SUM(input_tokens) as total_input_tokens,
   320→      SUM(output_tokens) as total_output_tokens,
   321→      SUM(total_tokens) as total_tokens,
   322→      AVG(success_score) as avg_success_score,
   323→      round((SUM(output_tokens) / 1000000.0) * ${OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION} +
   324→            (SUM(input_tokens) / 1000000.0) * ${INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION}, 2) as total_cost
   325→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   326→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   327→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   328→    GROUP BY user_id
   329→    ORDER BY total_cost DESC
   330→  `;
   331→
   332→	const result = await queryClickhouse<DeveloperBreakdownQueryResult>(query);
   333→
   334→	// Calculate total cost across all developers for cost_percentage
   335→	const grandTotalCost = result.reduce(
   336→		(sum, row) => sum + (Number(row.total_cost) || 0),
   337→		0,
   338→	);
   339→
   340→	return result.map((row) => {
   341→		const cost = Number(row.total_cost) || 0;
   342→
   343→		return {
   344→			user_id: row.user_id,
   345→			sessions: Number(row.total_sessions) || 0,
   346→			total_tokens: Number(row.total_tokens) || 0,
   347→			cost,
   348→			cost_percentage:
   349→				grandTotalCost > 0
   350→					? parseFloat(((cost / grandTotalCost) * 100).toFixed(2))
   351→					: 0,
   352→			avg_success_score: Number(row.avg_success_score) || 0,
   353→		};
   354→	});
   355→}

{"parentUuid":"16381af0-58eb-4d6c-9c12-9f3061d3ae85","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018SUUQqGHe3v47FGcfov8Z1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { ErrorTrendDataPoint, RecurringError } from "@rudel/api-routes";
     2→import {
     3→	buildDateFilter,
     4→	escapeString,
     5→	queryClickhouse,
     6→} from "../clickhouse.js";
     7→
     8→export interface CrossDeveloperError {
     9→	error_pattern: string;
    10→	developers_affected: number;
    11→	total_occurrences: number;
    12→	affected_user_ids: string[];
    13→	avg_session_duration_min: number;
    14→}
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Get top recurring errors across all sessions
    18→ */
    19→export async function getTopRecurringErrors(
    20→	orgId: string,
    21→	params: { days?: number; min_occurrences?: number; limit?: number } = {},
    22→): Promise<RecurringError[]> {
    23→	const { days = 7, min_occurrences = 2, limit = 15 } = params;
    24→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    25→	const d = Number(days);
    26→	const minOcc = Number(min_occurrences);
    27→	const lim = Number(limit);
    28→
    29→	const query = `
    30→    WITH error_sessions AS (
    31→      SELECT
    32→        session_id,
    33→        user_id,
    34→        session_date,
    35→        repository,
    36→        content,
    37→        CASE
    38→          WHEN content ILIKE '%Error:%' THEN extractAll(content, '([A-Z][a-zA-Z]+Error):')[1]
    39→          WHEN content ILIKE '%Exception:%' THEN extractAll(content, '([A-Z][a-zA-Z]+Exception):')[1]
    40→          WHEN content ILIKE '%failed%' THEN 'OperationFailed'
    41→          WHEN content ILIKE '%timeout%' THEN 'Timeout'
    42→          WHEN content ILIKE '%not found%' THEN 'NotFound'
    43→          ELSE 'UnknownError'
    44→        END as error_pattern
    45→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
    46→      WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
    47→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
    48→        AND (
    49→          content ILIKE '%error%' OR
    50→          content ILIKE '%exception%' OR
    51→          content ILIKE '%failed%' OR
    52→          content ILIKE '%timeout%'
    53→        )
    54→    )
    55→    SELECT
    56→      error_pattern,
    57→      COUNT(*) as occurrences,
    58→      uniq(session_id) as affected_sessions,
    59→      uniq(user_id) as affected_users,
    60→      max(session_date) as last_seen,
    61→      CASE
    62→        WHEN COUNT(*) >= 10 THEN 'high'
    63→        WHEN COUNT(*) >= 5 THEN 'medium'
    64→        ELSE 'low'
    65→      END as severity,
    66→      groupUniqArray(repository) as repositories
    67→    FROM error_sessions
    68→    WHERE error_pattern != ''
    69→    GROUP BY error_pattern
    70→    HAVING occurrences >= ${minOcc}
    71→    ORDER BY occurrences DESC
    72→    LIMIT ${lim}
    73→  `;
    74→
    75→	return queryClickhouse<RecurringError>(query);
    76→}
    77→
    78→/**
    79→ * Get errors affecting multiple developers
    80→ */
    81→export async function getCrossDeveloperErrors(
    82→	orgId: string,
    83→	params: { days?: number; min_developers?: number; limit?: number } = {},
    84→): Promise<CrossDeveloperError[]> {
    85→	const { days = 7, min_developers = 2, limit = 10 } = params;
    86→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    87→	const d = Number(days);
    88→	const minDevs = Number(min_developers);
    89→	const lim = Number(limit);
    90→
    91→	const query = `
    92→    SELECT
    93→      CASE
    94→        WHEN content ILIKE '%Error:%' THEN 'TypeError'
    95→        WHEN content ILIKE '%Exception:%' THEN 'Exception'
    96→        WHEN content ILIKE '%failed%' THEN 'OperationFailed'
    97→        WHEN content ILIKE '%timeout%' THEN 'Timeout'
    98→        ELSE 'UnknownError'
    99→      END as error_pattern,
   100→      uniq(user_id) as developers_affected,
   101→      COUNT(*) as total_occurrences,
   102→      groupUniqArray(user_id) as affected_user_ids,
   103→      round(AVG(actual_duration_min), 2) as avg_session_duration_min
   104→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   105→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   106→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   107→      AND (
   108→        content ILIKE '%error%' OR
   109→        content ILIKE '%exception%' OR
   110→        content ILIKE '%failed%' OR
   111→        content ILIKE '%timeout%'
   112→      )
   113→    GROUP BY error_pattern
   114→    HAVING developers_affected >= ${minDevs}
   115→    ORDER BY developers_affected DESC, total_occurrences DESC
   116→    LIMIT ${lim}
   117→  `;
   118→
   119→	return queryClickhouse<CrossDeveloperError>(query);
   120→}
   121→
   122→/**
   123→ * Get error metrics trends over time with various split options
   124→ */
   125→export async function getErrorTrends(
   126→	orgId: string,
   127→	params: {
   128→		start_date: string;
   129→		end_date: string;
   130→		split_by: "repository" | "user_id" | "model";
   131→	},
   132→): Promise<ErrorTrendDataPoint[]> {
   133→	const { start_date, end_date, split_by } = params;
   134→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   135→	const sd = escapeString(start_date);
   136→	const ed = escapeString(end_date);
   137→
   138→	if (split_by === "model") {
   139→		const query = `
   140→      WITH error_sessions AS (
   141→        SELECT
   142→          toDate(sa.session_date) as date,
   143→          sa.session_id,
   144→          sa.user_id,
   145→          sa.model_used as dimension_value,
   146→          sa.error_count
   147→        FROM rudel.session_analytics sa
   148→        WHERE sa.session_date >= '${sd}'
   149→          AND sa.session_date < '${ed}'
   150→          AND sa.organization_id = '${org}'
   151→          AND sa.error_count > 0
   152→      ),
   153→      daily_metrics AS (
   154→        SELECT
   155→          date,
   156→          dimension_value as dimension,
   157→          COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) as session_count,
   158→          SUM(error_count) as total_errors,
   159→          COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as interaction_count
   160→        FROM error_sessions
   161→        WHERE dimension IS NOT NULL AND dimension != '' AND dimension != 'unknown'
   162→        GROUP BY date, dimension
   163→      )
   164→      SELECT
   165→        date,
   166→        dimension,
   167→        round(total_errors / GREATEST(interaction_count, 1), 2) as avg_errors_per_interaction,
   168→        round(total_errors / GREATEST(session_count, 1), 2) as avg_errors_per_session,
   169→        total_errors
   170→      FROM daily_metrics
   171→      ORDER BY date, dimension
   172→    `;
   173→
   174→		return queryClickhouse<ErrorTrendDataPoint>(query);
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	// For repository and user_id splits
   178→	const dimension_field = split_by === "repository" ? "repository" : split_by;
   179→
   180→	const query = `
   181→    WITH error_sessions AS (
   182→      SELECT
   183→        toDate(session_date) as date,
   184→        session_id,
   185→        user_id,
   186→        ${dimension_field} as dimension_value,
   187→        length(splitByRegexp('\\\\n', content)) as error_count
   188→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   189→      WHERE session_date >= '${sd}'
   190→        AND session_date < '${ed}'
   191→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   192→        AND (
   193→          content ILIKE '%error%' OR
   194→          content ILIKE '%exception%' OR
   195→          content ILIKE '%failed%' OR
   196→          content ILIKE '%timeout%'
   197→        )
   198→    ),
   199→    daily_metrics AS (
   200→      SELECT
   201→        date,
   202→        dimension_value as dimension,
   203→        COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) as session_count,
   204→        SUM(error_count) as total_errors,
   205→        COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as interaction_count
   206→      FROM error_sessions
   207→      WHERE dimension IS NOT NULL AND dimension != ''
   208→      GROUP BY date, dimension
   209→    )
   210→    SELECT
   211→      date,
   212→      dimension,
   213→      round(total_errors / GREATEST(interaction_count, 1), 2) as avg_errors_per_interaction,
   214→      round(total_errors / GREATEST(session_count, 1), 2) as avg_errors_per_session,
   215→      total_errors
   216→    FROM daily_metrics
   217→    ORDER BY date, dimension
   218→  `;
   219→
   220→	return queryClickhouse<ErrorTrendDataPoint>(query);
   221→}
   222→"}]},"uuid":"d940f00c-1a67-422d-9025-5ee29396a4e1","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:17:03.385Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"16381af0-58eb-4d6c-9c12-9f3061d3ae85"}

task.prompt:
Analyze the Rudel codebase to understand the following areas. Give me concrete details about each:

1. **Session ingestion flow**: How are sessions uploaded? Look at the CLI upload command and the API ingestion endpoint. How are `ingested_at`, session dates, and session IDs handled?

2. **Token counting**: How are token counts computed? Search for "token" across the codebase, especially in the ClickHouse schema, materialized views, and any ingestion logic.

3. **Subagents/commands/skills tracking**: Is there any logic to extract and store subagent usage, commands, or skills from session transcripts?

4. **Learnings**: Is there any logic to extract "learnings" from session transcripts?

5. **Organization/invite system**: How does the org system work? Look at the auth setup, organization model, and any invite functionality.

6. **Dashboard queries**: What tables does the dashboard query? Is it using `claude_sessions` or `session_analytics`? Look at the web app's data fetching.

7. **Batch upload / historical sessions**: Is there any batch upload mechanism? How does the CLI handle multiple files?

8. **README / documentation**: What does the README say about where data goes (self-hosted vs hosted ClickHouse)?

Start from the root of the project at /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai

{"parentUuid":"22dc91ee-26d3-4461-ba6a-b8f679500b49","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { buildCommand } from "@stricli/core";
     2→import { classifySession } from "../lib/classifier.js";
     3→import { loadCredentials } from "../lib/credentials.js";
     4→import { getGitInfo } from "../lib/git-info.js";
     5→import { getProjectOrgId } from "../lib/project-config.js";
     6→import { resolveSession } from "../lib/session-resolver.js";
     7→import { readSubagentFiles } from "../lib/subagent-reader.js";
     8→import { extractAgentIds, readTranscript } from "../lib/transcript-reader.js";
     9→import {
    10→	DEFAULT_ENDPOINT,
    11→	type IngestRequest,
    12→	SESSION_TAGS,
    13→	type SessionTag,
    14→	type SubagentFile,
    15→} from "../lib/types.js";
    16→import { uploadSession } from "../lib/uploader.js";
    17→
    18→async function runUpload(
    19→	flags: {
    20→		tag?: SessionTag;
    21→		endpoint: string;
    22→		classify: boolean;
    23→		dryRun: boolean;
    24→		org?: string;
    25→	},
    26→	session: string,
    27→): Promise<void> {
    28→	const write = (msg: string) => {
    29→		process.stdout.write(`${msg}\n`);
    30→	};
    31→	const writeError = (msg: string) => {
    32→		process.stderr.write(`${msg}\n`);
    33→	};
    34→
    35→	// Load credentials
    36→	const credentials = loadCredentials();
    37→	if (!credentials && !flags.dryRun) {
    38→		writeError("Error: Not authenticated. Run `rudel login` first.");
    39→		process.exitCode = 1;
    40→		return;
    41→	}
    42→
    43→	// Resolve session (by ID or path)
    44→	write(`Resolving session: ${session}`);
    45→	let sessionInfo: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof resolveSession>>;
    46→	try {
    47→		sessionInfo = await resolveSession(session);
    48→	} catch (error) {
    49→		writeError(
    50→			`Error: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
    51→		);
    52→		process.exitCode = 1;
    53→		return;
    54→	}
    55→	write(`Found session at: ${sessionInfo.transcriptPath}`);
    56→
    57→	// Read transcript
    58→	write("Reading transcript...");
    59→	let content: string;
    60→	try {
    61→		content = await readTranscript(sessionInfo.transcriptPath);
    62→	} catch (error) {
    63→		writeError(
    64→			`Error reading transcript: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
    65→		);
    66→		process.exitCode = 1;
    67→		return;
    68→	}
    69→	write(`Transcript: ${content.length} bytes`);
    70→
    71→	// Extract and read subagents
    72→	const agentIds = extractAgentIds(content);
    73→	let subagents: SubagentFile[] = [];
    74→	if (agentIds.length > 0) {
    75→		write(`Found ${agentIds.length} subagent(s): ${agentIds.join(", ")}`);
    76→		subagents = await readSubagentFiles(
    77→			sessionInfo.sessionDir,
    78→			agentIds,
    79→			sessionInfo.sessionId,
    80→		);
    81→		write(
    82→			`Read ${subagents.length} subagent file(s): ${subagents.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s.content.length, 0)} bytes total`,
    83→		);
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	// Get git info
    87→	const gitInfo = await getGitInfo(sessionInfo.projectPath);
    88→	if (gitInfo.repository) write(`Repository: ${gitInfo.repository}`);
    89→	if (gitInfo.branch) write(`Branch: ${gitInfo.branch}`);
    90→
    91→	// Classify if requested
    92→	let tag = flags.tag;
    93→	if (!tag && flags.classify) {
    94→		write("Classifying session...");
    95→		tag = (await classifySession(content)) ?? undefined;
    96→		if (tag) write(`Classified as: ${tag}`);
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	// Resolve organization
   100→	const organizationId =
   101→		flags.org ?? (await getProjectOrgId(sessionInfo.projectPath));
   102→	if (organizationId) write(`Organization: ${organizationId}`);
   103→
   104→	// Build request
   105→	const request: IngestRequest = {
   106→		sessionId: sessionInfo.sessionId,
   107→		projectPath: sessionInfo.projectPath,
   108→		repository: gitInfo.repository,
   109→		gitBranch: gitInfo.branch,
   110→		gitSha: gitInfo.sha,
   111→		tag,
   112→		content,
   113→		subagents: subagents.length > 0 ? subagents : undefined,
   114→		organizationId,
   115→	};
   116→
   117→	// Upload or dry-run
   118→	if (flags.dryRun) {
   119→		const preview = {
   120→			...request,
   121→			content: `[${request.content.length} bytes]`,
   122→			subagents: request.subagents?.map((s) => ({
   123→				...s,
   124→				content: `[${s.content.length} bytes]`,
   125→			})),
   126→		};
   127→		write("Dry run - would upload:");
   128→		write(JSON.stringify(preview, null, 2));
   129→		return;
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	write("Uploading...");
   133→	const result = await uploadSession(request, {
   134→		endpoint: flags.endpoint,
   135→		// biome-ignore lint/style/noNonNullAssertion: validated above with early return
   136→		token: credentials!.token,
   137→	});
   138→
   139→	if (result.success) {
   140→		write("Upload successful!");
   141→	} else {
   142→		writeError(`Upload failed: ${result.error}`);
   143→		process.exitCode = 1;
   144→	}
   145→}
   146→
   147→export const uploadCommand = buildCommand({
   148→	loader: async () => ({ default: runUpload }),
   149→	parameters: {
   150→		positional: {
   151→			kind: "tuple",
   152→			parameters: [
   153→				{
   154→					brief: "Session ID or path to a session .jsonl file",
   155→					parse: String,
   156→					placeholder: "session",
   157→				},
   158→			],
   159→		},
   160→		flags: {
   161→			tag: {
   162→				kind: "enum",
   163→				values: [...SESSION_TAGS],
   164→				brief: "Session tag/category",
   165→				optional: true,
   166→			},
   167→			endpoint: {
   168→				kind: "parsed",
   169→				parse: String,
   170→				brief: "Override the upload endpoint URL",
   171→				default: DEFAULT_ENDPOINT,
   172→			},
   173→			classify: {
   174→				kind: "boolean",
   175→				brief: "Auto-classify session tag using Claude CLI",
   176→				default: false,
   177→			},
   178→			dryRun: {
   179→				kind: "boolean",
   180→				brief: "Preview what would be uploaded without sending",
   181→				default: false,
   182→			},
   183→			org: {
   184→				kind: "parsed",
   185→				parse: String,
   186→				brief: "Override the organization ID to upload to",
   187→				optional: true,
   188→			},
   189→		},
   190→		aliases: {
   191→			t: "tag",
   192→			c: "classify",
   193→			n: "dryRun",
   194→			o: "org",
   195→		},
   196→	},
   197→	docs: {
   198→		brief: "Upload a Claude Code session transcript to the backend",
   199→	},
   200→});

{"parentUuid":"7dc17787-9bb0-41b7-b537-9fa99c129975","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TKgP5qcLGLSCutaRtBzzgw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { IngestSessionInput } from "@rudel/api-routes";
     2→import type { Ingestor } from "@rudel/ch-schema";
     3→import {
     4→	ingestRudelClaudeSessions,
     5→	type RudelClaudeSessionsRow,
     6→} from "@rudel/ch-schema";
     7→
     8→interface IngestContext {
     9→	userId: string;
    10→	organizationId: string;
    11→}
    12→
    13→export function buildSessionRow(
    14→	input: IngestSessionInput,
    15→	context: IngestContext,
    16→): RudelClaudeSessionsRow {
    17→	const now = new Date().toISOString().replace("Z", "");
    18→
    19→	const subagents: Record<string, string> = {};
    20→	if (input.subagents) {
    21→		for (const sub of input.subagents) {
    22→			subagents[sub.agentId] = sub.content;
    23→		}
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	return {
    27→		session_date: now,
    28→		last_interaction_date: now,
    29→		session_id: input.sessionId,
    30→		organization_id: context.organizationId,
    31→		project_path: input.projectPath,
    32→		repository: input.repository ?? null,
    33→		content: input.content,
    34→		subagents,
    35→		skills: [],
    36→		slash_commands: [],
    37→		subagent_types: [],
    38→		ingested_at: now,
    39→		user_id: context.userId,
    40→		git_branch: input.gitBranch ?? null,
    41→		git_sha: input.gitSha ?? null,
    42→		input_tokens: "0",
    43→		output_tokens: "0",
    44→		cache_read_input_tokens: "0",
    45→		cache_creation_input_tokens: "0",
    46→		total_tokens: "0",
    47→		tag: input.tag ?? null,
    48→	};
    49→}
    50→
    51→export async function ingestSession(
    52→	ingestor: Ingestor,
    53→	input: IngestSessionInput,
    54→	context: IngestContext,
    55→): Promise<void> {
    56→	const row = buildSessionRow(input, context);
    57→	await ingestRudelClaudeSessions(ingestor, [row]);
    58→}
    59→"}]},"uuid":"0766b94a-90b4-416f-ae35-a3a3526ae232","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:15.283Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7dc17787-9bb0-41b7-b537-9fa99c129975"}

{"parentUuid":"03f74e37-e1c8-419f-a318-e2f15154007e","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0183E9jm8YM9HpDXFkvvERuR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { ORPCError } from "@orpc/server";
     2→import { member, organization } from "@rudel/sql-schema";
     3→import { eq } from "drizzle-orm";
     4→import { getClickhouse } from "./clickhouse.js";
     5→import { db } from "./db.js";
     6→import { analyticsRouter } from "./handlers/analytics/index.js";
     7→import { ingestSession } from "./ingest.js";
     8→import { authMiddleware, os } from "./middleware.js";
     9→
    10→const health = os.health.handler(() => {
    11→	return {
    12→		status: "ok" as const,
    13→		timestamp: Date.now(),
    14→	};
    15→});
    16→
    17→const me = os.me.use(authMiddleware).handler(({ context }) => {
    18→	return {
    19→		id: context.user.id,
    20→		email: context.user.email,
    21→		name: context.user.name,
    22→		image: context.user.image ?? null,
    23→		activeOrganizationId:
    24→			((context.session as Record<string, unknown>).activeOrganizationId as
    25→				| string
    26→				| null) ?? null,
    27→	};
    28→});
    29→
    30→const listMyOrganizations = os.listMyOrganizations
    31→	.use(authMiddleware)
    32→	.handler(async ({ context }) => {
    33→		const memberships = await db
    34→			.select({
    35→				id: organization.id,
    36→				name: organization.name,
    37→				slug: organization.slug,
    38→				logo: organization.logo,
    39→			})
    40→			.from(member)
    41→			.innerJoin(organization, eq(member.organizationId, organization.id))
    42→			.where(eq(member.userId, context.user.id));
    43→

    94→const rudel_session_analytics_mv = materializedView({
    95→	database: "rudel",
    96→	name: "session_analytics_mv",
    97→	to: { database: "rudel", name: "session_analytics" },
    98→	as: `
    99→  WITH
   100→    arrayFilter(
   101→      x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'type') IN ('user', 'assistant'),
   102→      splitByChar('\\n', cs.content)
   103→    ) AS _interaction_lines,
   104→
   105→    arrayFilter(x -> JSONHas(x, 'timestamp'), _interaction_lines) AS _ts_lines,
   106→
   107→    arrayMap(
   108→      x -> parseDateTime64BestEffort(JSONExtractString(x, 'timestamp')),
   109→      _ts_lines
   110→    ) AS _timestamps,
   111→
   112→    arrayMap(
   113→      x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'type'),
   114→      _ts_lines
   115→    ) AS _msg_types,
   116→
   117→    if(length(_timestamps) > 1,
   118→      arrayMap(i -> dateDiff('second', _timestamps[i], _timestamps[i+1]), range(1, length(_timestamps))),
   119→      []
   120→    ) AS _prompt_periods_sec,
   121→
   122→    if(length(_timestamps) > 1,
   123→      arrayMap(i -> if(_msg_types[i] = 'user' AND _msg_types[i+1] = 'assistant',
   124→        dateDiff('second', _timestamps[i], _timestamps[i+1]), 0), range(1, length(_timestamps))),
   125→      []
   126→    ) AS _inference_gaps,
   127→
   128→    if(length(_timestamps) > 1,
   129→      arrayMap(i -> if(_msg_types[i] = 'assistant' AND _msg_types[i+1] = 'user',
   130→        dateDiff('second', _timestamps[i], _timestamps[i+1]), 0), range(1, length(_timestamps))),
   131→      []
   132→    ) AS _human_gaps
   133→
   134→  SELECT
   135→    *,
   136→    toUInt32(length(_timestamps)) as total_interactions,
   137→    toUInt32(dateDiff('minute', arrayMin(_timestamps), arrayMax(_timestamps))) as actual_duration_min,
   138→    if(length(_prompt_periods_sec) > 0, round(arrayAvg(_prompt_periods_sec), 2), 0) as avg_period_sec,
   139→    if(
   140→      length(_prompt_periods_sec) > 0,
   141→      toFloat64(arrayElement(
   142→        arraySort(_prompt_periods_sec),
   143→        toUInt64(ceil(length(_prompt_periods_sec) / 2))
   144→      )),
   145→      0
   146→    ) as median_period_sec,
   147→    toUInt32(arrayCount(x -> x < 5, _prompt_periods_sec)) as quick_responses,
   148→    toUInt32(arrayCount(x -> x >= 5 AND x <= 60, _prompt_periods_sec)) as normal_responses,
   149→    toUInt32(arrayCount(x -> x > 300, _prompt_periods_sec)) as long_pauses,
   150→    toUInt32(
   151→      length(extractAll(cs.content, '"isApiErrorMessage":true'))
   152→      + length(extractAll(cs.content, '"is_error":true'))
   153→    ) as error_count,
   154→    JSONExtractString(
   155→      JSONExtractRaw(
   156→        arrayElement(
   157→          arrayFilter(
   158→            x -> JSONExtractString(x, 'type') = 'assistant',
   159→            splitByChar('\\n', cs.content)
   160→          ),
   161→          -1
   162→        ),
   163→        'message'
   164→      ),
   165→      'model'
   166→    ) as model_used,
   167→    toUInt8(if(cs.git_sha IS NOT NULL AND cs.git_sha != '', 1, 0)) as has_commit,
   168→    toUInt8(if(position(cs.content, '"name":"EnterPlanMode"') > 0, 1, 0)) as used_plan_mode,
   169→    toUInt32(arraySum(_inference_gaps)) as inference_duration_sec,
   170→    toUInt32(arraySum(_human_gaps)) as human_duration_sec,
   171→    CASE
   172→      WHEN dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) <= 10
   173→          AND cs.total_tokens < 500000
   174→          AND cs.output_tokens > 1000
   175→      THEN 'quick_win'
   176→      WHEN dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) > 30
   177→          AND cs.output_tokens > 50000
   178→          AND cs.git_sha IS NOT NULL AND cs.git_sha != ''
   179→      THEN 'deep_work'
   180→      WHEN cs.total_tokens > 1000000
   181→          AND (cs.output_tokens / nullif(cs.input_tokens, 0)) < 0.3
   182→          AND dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) > 20
   183→      THEN 'struggle'
   184→      WHEN length(cs.skills) >= 3
   185→          AND (cs.git_sha IS NULL OR cs.git_sha = '')
   186→          AND cs.total_tokens > 200000
   187→      THEN 'exploration'
   188→      WHEN dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) < 3
   189→          AND cs.output_tokens < 500
   190→      THEN 'abandoned'
   191→      ELSE 'standard'
   192→    END as session_archetype,
   193→    toUInt8(round(
   194→      50
   195→      + (if(cs.git_sha IS NOT NULL AND cs.git_sha != '', 20, 0))
   196→      + (if((cs.output_tokens / nullif(cs.input_tokens, 0)) > 0.5, 15, 0))
   197→      + (least(toUInt32(length(cs.skills)), 3) * 5)
   198→      - (if(cs.total_tokens > 1500000 AND (cs.git_sha IS NULL OR cs.git_sha = ''), 20, 0))
   199→      - (if(dateDiff('minute', cs.session_date, cs.last_interaction_date) < 2 AND cs.output_tokens < 200, 30, 0))
   200→      - (least(toUInt32(
   201→          length(extractAll(cs.content, '"isApiErrorMessage":true'))
   202→          + length(extractAll(cs.content, '"is_error":true'))
   203→        ), 10) * 2)
   204→    )) as success_score
   205→
   206→  FROM rudel.claude_sessions AS cs
   207→  WHERE length(_timestamps) > 0
   208→  QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cs.session_id ORDER BY cs.ingested_at DESC) = 1`,
   209→});
   210→
   211→export default schema(rudel_session_analytics, rudel_session_analytics_mv);
   212→"}]},"uuid":"c8eae69a-1766-41b8-b613-5f1dff098ae1","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:17.507Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"65135593-f397-41f5-911e-084e69a7adac"}

     4→export async function readTranscript(transcriptPath: string): Promise<string> {
     5→	const maxRetries = 5;
     6→	const delayMs = 500;
     7→
     8→	for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
     9→		try {
    10→			const file = Bun.file(transcriptPath);
    11→			if (!(await file.exists())) {
    12→				if (attempt < maxRetries) {
    13→					await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, delayMs));
    14→					continue;
    15→				}
    16→				throw new Error(
    17→					`Transcript file not found after ${maxRetries} attempts: ${transcriptPath}`,
    18→				);
    19→			}
    20→			return await file.text();
    21→		} catch (error) {
    22→			if (attempt < maxRetries) {
    23→				await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, delayMs));
    24→				continue;
    25→			}
    26→			throw error;
    27→		}
    28→	}
    29→
    30→	throw new Error(`Failed to read transcript: ${transcriptPath}`);
    31→}
    32→
    33→/**
    34→ * Extract agent IDs referenced in a session transcript.
    35→ * Agents are spawned via the Task tool and their IDs are recorded in toolUseResult.agentId
    36→ */
    37→export function extractAgentIds(sessionContent: string): string[] {
    38→	const agentIds = new Set<string>();
    39→
    40→	for (const line of sessionContent.split("\n")) {
    41→		if (!line.trim()) continue;
    42→
    43→		try {
    44→			const entry = JSON.parse(line);
    45→			if (entry.toolUseResult?.agentId) {
    46→				agentIds.add(entry.toolUseResult.agentId);
    47→			}
    48→		} catch {
    49→			// Skip malformed lines
    50→		}
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	return Array.from(agentIds);
    54→}
    55→"}]},"uuid":"6a3a76a5-7ab9-4b90-94c9-c63af5aac3d6","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:18.050Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"71ef691c-faf5-4b5d-beb2-ad92eff82073"}

{"parentUuid":"692b8f25-cd6b-4972-b1b9-ccb2135acb60","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MToLKfisViVqXnpfxwn134","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import * as schema from "@rudel/sql-schema";
     2→import { betterAuth } from "better-auth";
     3→import { drizzleAdapter } from "better-auth/adapters/drizzle";
     4→import { bearer, organization } from "better-auth/plugins";
     5→
     6→export interface AuthConfig {
     7→	appURL: string;
     8→	secret?: string;
     9→	socialProviders?: Record<string, { clientId: string; clientSecret: string }>;
    10→	trustedOrigins?: string[];
    11→}
    12→
    13→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any -- drizzleAdapter accepts { [key: string]: any }
    14→export function createAuth(db: object, config: AuthConfig) {
    15→	const trustedOrigins = config.trustedOrigins ?? [];
    16→	if (!trustedOrigins.includes(config.appURL)) {
    17→		trustedOrigins.push(config.appURL);
    18→	}
    19→
    20→	return betterAuth({
    21→		baseURL: config.appURL,
    22→		secret: config.secret,
    23→		database: drizzleAdapter(db as Parameters<typeof drizzleAdapter>[0], {
    24→			provider: "pg",
    25→			schema,
    26→		}),
    27→		emailAndPassword: {
    28→			enabled: true,
    29→		},
    30→		socialProviders: config.socialProviders,
    31→		plugins: [
    32→			bearer(),
    33→			organization({
    34→				allowUserToCreateOrganization: true,
    35→				creatorRole: "owner",
    36→			}),
    37→		],
    38→		session: {
    39→			expiresIn: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365,
    40→		},
    41→		trustedOrigins,
    42→		databaseHooks: {
    43→			user: {
    44→				create: {
    45→					after: async (user, ctx) => {
    46→						try {
    47→							const adapter = ctx?.context?.adapter;
    48→							if (!adapter) return;
    49→
    50→							const slug = `${user.email.split("@")[0]}-${user.id.slice(0, 8)}`;
    51→							const org = await adapter.create({
    52→								model: "organization",
    53→								data: {
    54→									id: user.id,
    55→									name: `${user.name}'s Workspace`,
    56→									slug,
    57→									createdAt: new Date(),
    58→								},
    59→								forceAllowId: true,
    60→							});
    61→
    62→							if (org) {
    63→								await adapter.create({
    64→									model: "member",
    65→									data: {
    66→										organizationId: org.id,
    67→										userId: user.id,
    68→										role: "owner",
    69→										createdAt: new Date(),
    70→									},
    71→								});
    72→							}
    73→						} catch (err) {
    74→							console.error(
    75→								"Failed to create default organization for user",
    76→								user.id,
    77→								err,
    78→							);
    79→						}
    80→					},
    81→				},
    82→			},
    83→		},
    84→	});
    85→}
    86→
    87→export type Auth = ReturnType<typeof createAuth>;
    88→export type Session = Auth["$Infer"]["Session"];
    89→"}]},"uuid":"e2ab2d86-5bd1-4226-b4bf-17804caf6b36","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:19.211Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"692b8f25-cd6b-4972-b1b9-ccb2135acb60"}

{"parentUuid":"22daac46-a53a-47cb-8bf9-989185c204af","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"REDACTED","toolUseID":"REDACTED","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:24.136Z","uuid":"2dc5ba42-2513-41df-9e69-b80711c05467"}
{"parentUuid":"22daac46-a53a-47cb-8bf9-989185c204af","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { readdir, stat } from "node:fs/promises";
     2→import { homedir } from "node:os";
     3→import { basename, dirname, join } from "node:path";
     4→
     5→const SESSIONS_BASE_DIR = join(homedir(), ".claude", "projects");
     6→
     7→export interface SessionInfo {
     8→	transcriptPath: string;
     9→	projectPath: string;
    10→	sessionDir: string;
    11→	sessionId: string;
    12→}
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Resolve a session from either a session ID or a full file path.
    16→ * Validates that the file is a main session (not a subagent file).
    17→ */
    18→export async function resolveSession(input: string): Promise<SessionInfo> {
    19→	const isPath = input.includes("/") || input.endsWith(".jsonl");
    20→
    21→	if (isPath) {
    22→		return resolveFromPath(input);
    23→	}
    24→	return resolveFromId(input);
    25→}
    26→
    27→async function resolveFromPath(filePath: string): Promise<SessionInfo> {
    28→	const filename = basename(filePath);
    29→	validateNotSubagent(filename);
    30→
    31→	const file = Bun.file(filePath);
    32→	if (!(await file.exists())) {
    33→		throw new Error(`Session file not found: ${filePath}`);
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	const sessionId = filename.replace(/\.jsonl$/, "");
    37→	const sessionDir = dirname(filePath);
    38→
    39→	// Walk up from sessionDir to find the project directory name
    40→	// Sessions live at: ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project-dir>/<sessionId>.jsonl
    41→	const parentDir = basename(sessionDir);
    42→	const projectPath = await decodeProjectPath(parentDir);
    43→
    44→	return { transcriptPath: filePath, projectPath, sessionDir, sessionId };
    45→}
    46→
    47→async function resolveFromId(sessionId: string): Promise<SessionInfo> {
    48→	validateNotSubagent(`${sessionId}.jsonl`);
    49→	const sessionFileName = `${sessionId}.jsonl`;
    50→
    51→	let projectDirs: string[];
    52→	try {
    53→		projectDirs = await readdir(SESSIONS_BASE_DIR);
    54→	} catch {
    55→		throw new Error(`Session not found: ${sessionId}`);
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	for (const projectDir of projectDirs) {
    59→		const sessionDir = join(SESSIONS_BASE_DIR, projectDir);
    60→		try {
    61→			const files = await readdir(sessionDir);
    62→			if (files.includes(sessionFileName)) {
    63→				const transcriptPath = join(sessionDir, sessionFileName);
    64→				const projectPath = await decodeProjectPath(projectDir);
    65→				return {
    66→					transcriptPath,
    67→					projectPath,
    68→					sessionDir,
    69→					sessionId,
    70→				};
    71→			}
    72→		} catch {}
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	throw new Error(`Session not found: ${sessionId}`);
    76→}
    77→
    78→function validateNotSubagent(filename: string): void {
    79→	if (filename.startsWith("agent-") && filename.endsWith(".jsonl")) {
    80→		throw new Error(
    81→			"This is a subagent file, not a main session. Please provide the main session ID or path.",
    82→		);
    83→	}
    84→}
    85→
    86→/**
    87→ * Decode an encoded project directory name back to the actual filesystem path.
    88→ * Handles directory names that contain dashes by checking if paths exist.
    89→ *
    90→ * Example: "-Users-marc-Workspace-claude-marketplace" -> "/Users/marc/Workspace/claude-marketplace"
    91→ */
    92→async function decodeProjectPath(encodedDir: string): Promise<string> {
    93→	const parts = encodedDir.replace(/^-/, "").split("-");
    94→
    95→	async function findPath(
    96→		partIndex: number,
    97→		currentPath: string,
    98→	): Promise<string | null> {
    99→		if (partIndex >= parts.length) {
   100→			try {
   101→				await stat(currentPath);
   102→				return currentPath;
   103→			} catch {
   104→				return null;
   105→			}
   106→		}
   107→
   108→		for (let endIndex = parts.length; endIndex > partIndex; endIndex--) {
   109→			const segment = parts.slice(partIndex, endIndex).join("-");
   110→			const testPath = currentPath
   111→				? `${currentPath}/${segment}`
   112→				: `/${segment}`;
   113→
   114→			try {
   115→				await stat(testPath);
   116→				if (endIndex === parts.length) {
   117→					return testPath;
   118→				}
   119→				const result = await findPath(endIndex, testPath);
   120→				if (result) {
   121→					return result;
   122→				}
   123→			} catch {
   124→				// Path doesn't exist, try shorter segment
   125→			}
   126→		}
   127→
   128→		return null;
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	const result = await findPath(0, "");
   132→	if (result) {
   133→		return result;
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	// Fallback: simple dash-to-slash replacement
   137→	return `/${parts.join("/")}`;
   138→}
   139→"}]},"uuid":"cd9c8a0b-84ec-4ef6-9e38-56a5ac38b05e","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:24.135Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"22daac46-a53a-47cb-8bf9-989185c204af"}

{"parentUuid":"770d6a97-1518-4321-83d7-2a6fbacd8061","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01T4tmxXanezWZES6Dfeu9u6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { join } from "node:path";
     2→import type { SubagentFile } from "./types.js";
     3→
     4→/**
     5→ * Read all subagent files for a session.
     6→ * Subagent transcripts can be stored in multiple locations:
     7→ * 1. {sessionDir}/agent-{agentId}.jsonl (legacy)
     8→ * 2. {sessionDir}/{sessionId}/subagents/agent-{agentId}.jsonl (new format)
     9→ */
    10→export async function readSubagentFiles(
    11→	sessionDir: string,
    12→	agentIds: string[],
    13→	sessionId?: string,
    14→): Promise<SubagentFile[]> {
    15→	const subagents: SubagentFile[] = [];
    16→
    17→	for (const agentId of agentIds) {
    18→		const possiblePaths = [
    19→			join(sessionDir, `agent-${agentId}.jsonl`),
    20→			...(sessionId
    21→				? [join(sessionDir, sessionId, "subagents", `agent-${agentId}.jsonl`)]
    22→				: []),
    23→		];
    24→
    25→		for (const agentPath of possiblePaths) {
    26→			try {
    27→				const file = Bun.file(agentPath);
    28→				if (await file.exists()) {
    29→					const content = await file.text();
    30→					subagents.push({ agentId, content });
    31→					break;
    32→				}
    33→			} catch {
    34→				// Try next path
    35→			}
    36→		}
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	return subagents;
    40→}
    41→"}]},"uuid":"d3d3df2f-ff10-4b8b-bccb-3f7105d62ffb","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:24.711Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"770d6a97-1518-4321-83d7-2a6fbacd8061"}

{"parentUuid":"ecedc60e-1abe-472f-b6a2-1af91a12ce85","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Qy1Zoz8EUbK31TzUut3qzi","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { mkdir, unlink } from "node:fs/promises";
     2→import { homedir } from "node:os";
     3→import { join } from "node:path";
     4→import { SESSION_TAGS, type SessionTag } from "./types.js";
     5→
     6→const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a session classifier. Analyze the Claude Code session transcript and classify it into exactly ONE of these categories:
     7→
     8→- research: Exploring codebase, understanding code, answering questions about how things work
     9→- new_feature: Implementing new functionality or features
    10→- bug_fix: Fixing bugs, errors, or unexpected behavior
    11→- refactoring: Restructuring existing code without changing functionality
    12→- documentation: Writing or updating documentation, comments, READMEs
    13→- tests: Writing, updating, or fixing tests
    14→
    15→CRITICAL: Respond with ONLY the tag name. Nothing else. No explanation, no punctuation, no formatting. Just ONE of: research, new_feature, bug_fix, refactoring, documentation, tests`;
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Classify a session transcript using Claude CLI.
    19→ * Returns one of the predefined tags based on the session content.
    20→ */
    21→export async function classifySession(
    22→	content: string,
    23→): Promise<SessionTag | undefined> {
    24→	// Truncate content to avoid excessive API costs
    25→	const truncatedContent = content.slice(0, 50000);
    26→
    27→	const tempDir = join(homedir(), ".claude", "temp");
    28→	const tempFile = join(tempDir, `classify-${Date.now()}.txt`);
    29→
    30→	try {
    31→		await mkdir(tempDir, { recursive: true });
    32→		await Bun.write(
    33→			tempFile,
    34→			`Classify this session transcript:\n\n${truncatedContent}`,
    35→		);
    36→
    37→		const prompt = `Read and classify the session transcript in this file: ${tempFile}`;
    38→		const escapedPrompt = prompt.replace(/'/g, "'\\''");
    39→		const escapedSystemPrompt = SYSTEM_PROMPT.replace(/'/g, "'\\''");
    40→
    41→		const proc = Bun.spawn(
    42→			[
    43→				"sh",
    44→				"-c",
    45→				`echo '${escapedPrompt}' | claude --output-format text --print --model haiku --no-session-persistence --dangerously-skip-permissions --system-prompt '${escapedSystemPrompt}'`,
    46→			],
    47→			{ stdout: "pipe", stderr: "pipe" },
    48→		);
    49→
    50→		const exitCode = await proc.exited;
    51→		const stdout = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();
    52→
    53→		if (exitCode !== 0) {
    54→			return "other";
    55→		}
    56→
    57→		const output = stdout.trim().toLowerCase();
    58→
    59→		// Exact match
    60→		if (SESSION_TAGS.includes(output as SessionTag)) {
    61→			return output as SessionTag;
    62→		}
    63→
    64→		// Search for valid tags in verbose output
    65→		for (const tag of SESSION_TAGS) {
    66→			if (new RegExp(`\\b${tag}\\b`).test(output)) {
    67→				return tag;
    68→			}
    69→		}
    70→
    71→		return "other";
    72→	} catch {
    73→		return undefined;
    74→	} finally {
    75→		try {
    76→			await unlink(tempFile);
    77→		} catch {
    78→			// Ignore cleanup errors
    79→		}
    80→	}
    81→}
    82→"}]},"uuid":"79d2fad7-78db-48c2-bbaf-5e189700b9e2","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:25.198Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ecedc60e-1abe-472f-b6a2-1af91a12ce85"}

{"parentUuid":"7156905d-bb5e-46a4-8f4e-9ad779a932f4","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PrEhtkwGAfasttZqmRekX1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { buildCommand } from "@stricli/core";
     2→import { loadCredentials } from "../../../lib/credentials.js";
     3→import { getGitInfo } from "../../../lib/git-info.js";
     4→import { getProjectOrgId } from "../../../lib/project-config.js";
     5→import { readSubagentFiles } from "../../../lib/subagent-reader.js";
     6→import {
     7→	extractAgentIds,
     8→	readTranscript,
     9→} from "../../../lib/transcript-reader.js";
    10→import type { IngestRequest } from "../../../lib/types.js";
    11→import { uploadSession } from "../../../lib/uploader.js";
    12→
    13→interface HookInput {
    14→	session_id: string;
    15→	transcript_path: string;
    16→	cwd: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→async function readStdin(): Promise<string> {
    20→	const chunks: string[] = [];
    21→	for await (const chunk of process.stdin) {
    22→		chunks.push(typeof chunk === "string" ? chunk : chunk.toString());
    23→	}
    24→	return chunks.join("");
    25→}
    26→
    27→async function runSessionEnd(): Promise<void> {
    28→	try {
    29→		const raw = await readStdin();
    30→		if (!raw.trim()) return;
    31→
    32→		const input = JSON.parse(raw) as HookInput;
    33→		if (!input.session_id || !input.transcript_path) return;
    34→
    35→		const credentials = loadCredentials();
    36→		if (!credentials) return;
    37→
    38→		const content = await readTranscript(input.transcript_path);
    39→
    40→		const agentIds = extractAgentIds(content);
    41→		const { dirname } = await import("node:path");
    42→		const sessionDir = dirname(input.transcript_path);
    43→		const subagents =
    44→			agentIds.length > 0
    45→				? await readSubagentFiles(sessionDir, agentIds, input.session_id)
    46→				: [];
    47→
    48→		const gitInfo = await getGitInfo(input.cwd);
    49→		const organizationId = await getProjectOrgId(input.cwd);
    50→
    51→		const request: IngestRequest = {
    52→			sessionId: input.session_id,
    53→			projectPath: input.cwd,
    54→			repository: gitInfo.repository,
    55→			gitBranch: gitInfo.branch,
    56→			gitSha: gitInfo.sha,
    57→			content,
    58→			subagents: subagents.length > 0 ? subagents : undefined,
    59→			organizationId,
    60→		};
    61→
    62→		const apiBase = process.env.RUDEL_API_BASE ?? credentials.apiBaseUrl;
    63→		const endpoint = `${apiBase}/rpc`;
    64→		await uploadSession(request, { endpoint, token: credentials.token });
    65→	} catch {
    66→		// Swallow all errors — this runs async in the background
    67→	}
    68→}
    69→
    70→export const sessionEndCommand = buildCommand({
    71→	loader: async () => ({ default: runSessionEnd }),
    72→	parameters: {},
    73→	docs: {
    74→		brief: "Handle Claude Code SessionEnd hook",
    75→	},
    76→});
    77→"}]},"uuid":"a0522afa-cc1a-4d9a-bd08-2cd0a6c7db97","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:26.489Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7156905d-bb5e-46a4-8f4e-9ad779a932f4"}

{"parentUuid":"78e5e815-c0b4-4a86-8cee-e7ff408fef7e","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"REDACTED","toolUseID":"REDACTED","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:31.886Z","uuid":"b3fc97bc-2192-43c9-9650-bf819340e8ed"}
{"parentUuid":"78e5e815-c0b4-4a86-8cee-e7ff408fef7e","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type {
     2→	ModelTokensTrendData,
     3→	OverviewKPIs,
     4→	UsageTrendData,
     5→} from "@rudel/api-routes";
     6→import {
     7→	buildDateFilter,
     8→	escapeString,
     9→	queryClickhouse,
    10→} from "../clickhouse.js";
    11→
    12→export interface Insight {
    13→	type: "trend" | "performer" | "alert" | "info";
    14→	severity: "positive" | "warning" | "negative" | "info";
    15→	message: string;
    16→	link: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Get overview KPI counts: distinct users, sessions, projects, subagents, skills, slash commands
    21→ */
    22→export async function getOverviewKPIs(
    23→	orgId: string,
    24→	days = 7,
    25→): Promise<OverviewKPIs> {
    26→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    27→	const dateFilter = buildDateFilter(days);
    28→
    29→	const query = `
    30→    SELECT
    31→      uniq(user_id) as distinct_users,
    32→      count() as distinct_sessions,
    33→      uniq(project_path) as distinct_projects,
    34→      (SELECT uniqExact(val) FROM rudel.session_analytics ARRAY JOIN subagent_types as val WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)} AND organization_id = '${org}' AND val != '') as distinct_subagents,
    35→      (SELECT uniqExact(val) FROM rudel.session_analytics ARRAY JOIN skills as val WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)} AND organization_id = '${org}' AND val != '') as distinct_skills,
    36→      (SELECT uniqExact(val) FROM rudel.session_analytics ARRAY JOIN slash_commands as val WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)} AND organization_id = '${org}' AND val != '') as distinct_slash_commands
    37→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
    38→    WHERE ${dateFilter}
    39→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
    40→  `;
    41→
    42→	const result = await queryClickhouse<OverviewKPIs>(query);
    43→	return (
    44→		result[0] || {
    45→			distinct_users: 0,
    46→			distinct_sessions: 0,
    47→			distinct_projects: 0,
    48→			distinct_subagents: 0,
    49→			distinct_skills: 0,
    50→			distinct_slash_commands: 0,
    51→		}
    52→	);
    53→}
    54→
    55→/**
    56→ * Get token usage broken down by model and day
    57→ */
    58→export async function getModelTokensTrend(
    59→	orgId: string,
    60→	days = 30,
    61→): Promise<ModelTokensTrendData[]> {
    62→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    63→	const dateFilter = buildDateFilter(days);
    64→
    65→	const query = `
    66→    SELECT
    67→      toDate(session_date) as date,
    68→      model_used as model,
    69→      sum(total_tokens) as total_tokens,
    70→      sum(input_tokens) as input_tokens,
    71→      sum(output_tokens) as output_tokens
    72→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
    73→    WHERE ${dateFilter}
    74→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
    75→      AND model_used != ''
    76→      AND model_used != 'unknown'
    77→    GROUP BY date, model
    78→    ORDER BY date ASC, total_tokens DESC
    79→  `;
    80→
    81→	return queryClickhouse<ModelTokensTrendData>(query);
    82→}
    83→
    84→/**
    85→ * Get detailed usage trend data aggregated by day
    86→ */
    87→export async function getUsageTrendDetailed(
    88→	orgId: string,
    89→	days = 30,
    90→): Promise<UsageTrendData[]> {
    91→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    92→	const dateFilter = buildDateFilter(days);
    93→
    94→	const query = `
    95→    SELECT
    96→      toDate(session_date) as date,
    97→      count() as sessions,
    98→      uniq(user_id) as active_users,
    99→      round(sum(actual_duration_min) / 60, 2) as total_hours,
   100→      sum(total_tokens) as total_tokens
   101→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   102→    WHERE ${dateFilter}
   103→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   104→    GROUP BY date
   105→    ORDER BY date ASC
   106→  `;
   107→
   108→	return queryClickhouse<UsageTrendData>(query);
   109→}
   110→
   111→/**
   112→ * Generate rule-based insights from current data
   113→ */
   114→export async function getOverviewInsights(
   115→	orgId: string,
   116→	days = 7,
   117→): Promise<Insight[]> {
   118→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   119→	const insights: Insight[] = [];
   120→
   121→	const currentPeriodQuery = `
   122→    SELECT
   123→      count() as total_sessions,
   124→      uniq(user_id) as total_users,
   125→      round(avg(actual_duration_min), 2) as avg_duration_min
   126→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   127→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)}
   128→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   129→  `;
   130→
   131→	const previousDays = Number(days) * 2;
   132→	const previousPeriodQuery = `
   133→    SELECT
   134→      count() as total_sessions,
   135→      uniq(user_id) as total_users,
   136→      round(avg(actual_duration_min), 2) as avg_duration_min
   137→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   138→    WHERE session_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${previousDays} DAY
   139→      AND session_date < now64(3) - INTERVAL ${Number(days)} DAY
   140→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   141→  `;
   142→
   143→	interface PeriodStats {
   144→		total_sessions: number;
   145→		total_users: number;
   146→		avg_duration_min: number;
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	const [currentData, previousData] = await Promise.all([
   150→		queryClickhouse<PeriodStats>(currentPeriodQuery),
   151→		queryClickhouse<PeriodStats>(previousPeriodQuery),
   152→	]);
   153→
   154→	const current = currentData[0];
   155→	const previous = previousData[0] || { total_sessions: 0 };
   156→
   157→	// Insight 1: Session trend analysis
   158→	if (current && previous) {
   159→		const sessionChange =
   160→			previous.total_sessions > 0
   161→				? ((current.total_sessions - previous.total_sessions) /
   162→						previous.total_sessions) *
   163→					100
   164→				: 0;
   165→
   166→		if (Math.abs(sessionChange) >= 10) {
   167→			const direction = sessionChange > 0 ? "up" : "down";
   168→			const severity = sessionChange > 0 ? "positive" : "warning";
   169→			insights.push({
   170→				type: "trend",
   171→				severity,
   172→				message: `Team activity ${direction} ${Math.abs(sessionChange).toFixed(1)}% this week`,
   173→				link: "/dashboard/team",
   174→			});
   175→		}
   176→	}
   177→
   178→	// Insight 2: Top performer identification
   179→	const topPerformerQuery = `
   180→    SELECT
   181→      user_id,
   182→      count() as sessions,
   183→      round(sum(actual_duration_min) / 60, 1) as total_hours
   184→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   185→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)}
   186→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   187→    GROUP BY user_id
   188→    ORDER BY sessions DESC
   189→    LIMIT 1
   190→  `;
   191→
   192→	const topPerformer = await queryClickhouse<{
   193→		user_id: string;
   194→		sessions: number;
   195→		total_hours: number;
   196→	}>(topPerformerQuery);
   197→	if (topPerformer.length > 0 && topPerformer[0]) {
   198→		const performer = topPerformer[0];
   199→		const { getUserMapping } = await import("./user.service.js");
   200→		const userMapping = await getUserMapping(orgId, performer.user_id, days);
   201→		const displayName =
   202→			userMapping?.username || `${performer.user_id.substring(0, 8)}...`;
   203→
   204→		insights.push({
   205→			type: "performer",
   206→			severity: "info",
   207→			message: `Top contributor: ${displayName} (${performer.sessions} sessions, ${performer.total_hours}h)`,
   208→			link: `/dashboard/developers/${performer.user_id}`,
   209→		});
   210→	}
   211→
   212→	// Insight 3: Knowledge silos detection
   213→	const knowledgeSilosQuery = `
   214→    SELECT
   215→      project_path,
   216→      uniq(user_id) as unique_users,
   217→      count() as sessions
   218→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   219→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(30)}
   220→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   221→    GROUP BY project_path
   222→    HAVING unique_users = 1 AND sessions >= 5
   223→    ORDER BY sessions DESC
   224→  `;
   225→
   226→	const silos = await queryClickhouse<{
   227→		project_path: string;
   228→		unique_users: number;
   229→		sessions: number;
   230→	}>(knowledgeSilosQuery);
   231→	if (silos.length > 0) {
   232→		insights.push({
   233→			type: "alert",
   234→			severity: "warning",
   235→			message: `${silos.length} project${silos.length > 1 ? "s" : ""} with only one developer - risk of knowledge silos`,
   236→			link: "/dashboard/projects",
   237→		});
   238→	}
   239→
   240→	return insights.slice(0, 3);
   241→}
   242→
   243→export interface TeamSummaryPeriodData {
   244→	total_sessions: number;
   245→	active_users: number;
   246→	avg_duration_min: number;
   247→	avg_sessions_per_user: number;
   248→}
   249→
   250→/**
   251→ * Get team summary with previous period comparison
   252→ */
   253→export async function getTeamSummaryWithComparison(orgId: string, days = 7) {
   254→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   255→	const previousDays = Number(days) * 2;
   256→
   257→	const currentQuery = `
   258→    SELECT
   259→      count() as total_sessions,
   260→      uniq(user_id) as active_users,
   261→      round(avg(actual_duration_min), 2) as avg_duration_min,
   262→      round(count() / uniq(user_id), 2) as avg_sessions_per_user
   263→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   264→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)}
   265→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   266→  `;
   267→
   268→	const previousQuery = `
   269→    SELECT
   270→      count() as total_sessions,
   271→      uniq(user_id) as active_users,
   272→      round(avg(actual_duration_min), 2) as avg_duration_min,
   273→      round(count() / uniq(user_id), 2) as avg_sessions_per_user
   274→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   275→    WHERE session_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${previousDays} DAY
   276→      AND session_date < now64(3) - INTERVAL ${Number(days)} DAY
   277→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   278→  `;
   279→
   280→	const [currentData, previousData] = await Promise.all([
   281→		queryClickhouse<TeamSummaryPeriodData>(currentQuery),
   282→		queryClickhouse<TeamSummaryPeriodData>(previousQuery),
   283→	]);
   284→
   285→	const defaultPeriod: TeamSummaryPeriodData = {
   286→		total_sessions: 0,
   287→		active_users: 0,
   288→		avg_duration_min: 0,
   289→		avg_sessions_per_user: 0,
   290→	};
   291→
   292→	const current = currentData[0] || defaultPeriod;
   293→	const previous = previousData[0] || defaultPeriod;
   294→
   295→	const calculateChange = (curr: number, prev: number) => {
   296→		if (!prev || prev === 0) return 0;
   297→		return ((curr - prev) / prev) * 100;
   298→	};
   299→
   300→	const changes = {
   301→		total_sessions: calculateChange(
   302→			current.total_sessions || 0,
   303→			previous.total_sessions || 0,
   304→		),
   305→		active_users: calculateChange(
   306→			current.active_users || 0,
   307→			previous.active_users || 0,
   308→		),
   309→		avg_duration_min: calculateChange(
   310→			current.avg_duration_min || 0,
   311→			previous.avg_duration_min || 0,
   312→		),
   313→		avg_sessions_per_user: calculateChange(
   314→			current.avg_sessions_per_user || 0,
   315→			previous.avg_sessions_per_user || 0,
   316→		),
   317→	};
   318→
   319→	return { current, previous, changes };
   320→}
   321→
   322→/**
   323→ * Get session success rate metrics with comparison
   324→ */
   325→export async function getSuccessRateMetrics(orgId: string, days = 7) {
   326→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   327→	const previousDays = Number(days) * 2;
   328→
   329→	const currentQuery = `
   330→    SELECT
   331→      count() as total_sessions,
   332→      round(avg(success_score), 1) as avg_success_score,
   333→      countIf(success_score >= 70) as high_quality_sessions
   334→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   335→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(days)}
   336→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   337→  `;
   338→
   339→	const previousQuery = `
   340→    SELECT
   341→      count() as total_sessions,
   342→      round(avg(success_score), 1) as avg_success_score,
   343→      countIf(success_score >= 70) as high_quality_sessions
   344→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   345→    WHERE session_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${previousDays} DAY
   346→      AND session_date < now64(3) - INTERVAL ${Number(days)} DAY
   347→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   348→  `;
   349→
   350→	interface SuccessRateStats {
   351→		total_sessions: number;
   352→		avg_success_score: number;
   353→		high_quality_sessions: number;
   354→	}
   355→
   356→	const [currentData, previousData] = await Promise.all([
   357→		queryClickhouse<SuccessRateStats>(currentQuery),
   358→		queryClickhouse<SuccessRateStats>(previousQuery),
   359→	]);
   360→
   361→	const current: SuccessRateStats = currentData[0] || {
   362→		total_sessions: 0,
   363→		avg_success_score: 0,
   364→		high_quality_sessions: 0,
   365→	};
   366→
   367→	const previous: SuccessRateStats = previousData[0] || {
   368→		total_sessions: 0,
   369→		avg_success_score: 0,
   370→		high_quality_sessions: 0,
   371→	};
   372→
   373→	const currentSuccessRate =
   374→		current.total_sessions > 0
   375→			? (current.high_quality_sessions / current.total_sessions) * 100
   376→			: 0;
   377→
   378→	const previousSuccessRate =
   379→		previous.total_sessions > 0
   380→			? (previous.high_quality_sessions / previous.total_sessions) * 100
   381→			: 0;
   382→
   383→	return {
   384→		current: {
   385→			high_quality_sessions: current.high_quality_sessions,
   386→			total_sessions: current.total_sessions,
   387→			success_rate: currentSuccessRate,
   388→		},
   389→		previous: {
   390→			high_quality_sessions: previous.high_quality_sessions,
   391→			total_sessions: previous.total_sessions,
   392→			success_rate: previousSuccessRate,
   393→		},
   394→		changes: {
   395→			success_rate: currentSuccessRate - previousSuccessRate,
   396→		},
   397→	};
   398→}
   399→"}]},"uuid":"6acb70ac-919d-4d40-87c7-483ac2296e91","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:31.886Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"78e5e815-c0b4-4a86-8cee-e7ff408fef7e"}

{"parentUuid":"605a3962-ab73-4b2d-aaa8-5788c5ab3fa7","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QfMhQiYfQFGd5Hn5KWtUP7","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type {
