    58→export interface SessionAnalyticsSummary extends SessionAnalyticsSummaryBase {
    59→	total_interactions: number;

    61→	median_response_time_sec: number;
    62→	quick_response_rate: number;
    63→	long_pause_rate: number;

    65→
    66→/**
    67→ * Get session analytics from the materialized view
    68→ */
    69→export async function getSessionAnalytics(
    70→	orgId: string,
    71→	params: {
    72→		days?: number;
    73→		user_id?: string;
    74→		project_path?: string;
    75→		repository?: string;
    76→		limit?: number;
    77→		offset?: number;
    78→		sort_by?: "date" | "duration" | "interactions";
    79→		sort_order?: "asc" | "desc";
    80→	} = {},
    81→): Promise<SessionAnalytics[]> {
    82→	const {
    83→		days = 30,
    84→		user_id,
    85→		project_path,
    86→		repository,
    87→		limit = 50,
    88→		offset = 0,
    89→		sort_by = "date",
    90→		sort_order = "desc",
    91→	} = params;
    92→
    93→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    94→	const d = Number(days);
    95→	const lim = Number(limit);
    96→	const off = Number(offset);
    97→
    98→	let filters = "";
    99→	if (user_id) {
   100→		filters += ` AND user_id = '${escapeString(user_id)}'`;
   101→	}
   102→	if (project_path) {
   103→		filters += ` AND project_path = '${escapeString(project_path)}'`;
   104→	}
   105→	if (repository) {
   106→		filters += ` AND repository = '${escapeString(repository)}'`;
   107→	}
   108→
   109→	const sortColumn =
   110→		sort_by === "duration"
   111→			? "actual_duration_min"
   112→			: sort_by === "interactions"
   113→				? "total_interactions"
   114→				: "sa.session_date";
   115→	const sortDirection = sort_order === "asc" ? "ASC" : "DESC";
   116→
   117→	const query = `
   118→    SELECT
   119→      session_id,
   120→      user_id,
   121→      formatDateTime(sa.session_date, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%i:%S') as session_date,
   122→      project_path,
   123→      organization_id,
   124→      repository,
   125→      total_interactions,
   126→      avg_period_sec,
   127→      median_period_sec,
   128→      quick_responses,
   129→      normal_responses,
   130→      long_pauses,
   131→      actual_duration_min,
   132→      formatDateTime(sa.last_interaction_date, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%i:%S') as last_interaction_date,
   133→      total_tokens,
   134→      input_tokens,
   135→      output_tokens,
   136→      git_sha,
   137→      git_branch,
   138→      has_commit,
   139→      subagent_types,
   140→      skills,
   141→      slash_commands,
   142→      session_archetype,
   143→      success_score,
   144→      error_count,
   145→      model_used,
   146→      used_plan_mode
   147→    FROM rudel.session_analytics sa
   148→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d, "sa.session_date")}
   149→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   150→      ${filters}
   151→    ORDER BY ${sortColumn} ${sortDirection}
   152→    LIMIT ${lim}
   153→    OFFSET ${off}
   154→  `;
   155→
   156→	const raw = await queryClickhouse<SessionAnalyticsRaw>(query);
   157→
   158→	return raw.map(
   159→		(row): SessionAnalytics => ({
   160→			session_id: row.session_id,
   161→			user_id: row.user_id,
   162→			session_date: row.session_date,
   163→			project_path: row.project_path,
   164→			repository: row.repository || null,
   165→			duration_min: row.actual_duration_min,
   166→			total_tokens: row.total_tokens,
   167→			input_tokens: row.input_tokens,
   168→			output_tokens: row.output_tokens,
   169→			success_score: row.success_score,
   170→			total_interactions: row.total_interactions,
   171→			avg_period_sec: row.avg_period_sec,
   172→			subagent_types: row.subagent_types,
   173→			skills: row.skills,
   174→			slash_commands: row.slash_commands,
   175→			has_commit: row.has_commit > 0,
   176→			session_archetype: row.session_archetype,
   177→			model_used: row.model_used,
   178→			used_plan_mode: row.used_plan_mode > 0,
   179→		}),
   180→	);
   181→}
   182→
   183→/**
   184→ * Get summary statistics from session analytics
   185→ */
   186→export async function getSessionAnalyticsSummary(
   187→	orgId: string,
   188→	params: {
   189→		days?: number;
   190→		user_id?: string;
   191→		project_path?: string;
   192→	} = {},
   193→): Promise<SessionAnalyticsSummary> {
   194→	const { days = 30, user_id, project_path } = params;
   195→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   196→	const d = Number(days);
   197→
   198→	let filters = "";
   199→	if (user_id) {
   200→		filters += ` AND user_id = '${escapeString(user_id)}'`;
   201→	}
   202→	if (project_path) {
   203→		filters += ` AND project_path = '${escapeString(project_path)}'`;
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	const query = `
   207→    WITH totals AS (
   208→      SELECT
   209→        COUNT(*) as cnt_sessions,
   210→        SUM(total_interactions) as sum_interactions,
   211→        SUM(quick_responses) as sum_quick_responses,
   212→        SUM(long_pauses) as sum_long_pauses,
   213→        ifNull(AVG(actual_duration_min), 0) as avg_duration,
   214→        ifNull(AVG(total_interactions), 0) as avg_interactions,
   215→        ifNull(AVG(avg_period_sec), 0) as avg_response,
   216→        ifNull(AVG(median_period_sec), 0) as med_response,
   217→        countIf(length(subagent_types) > 0) as cnt_subagents,
   218→        countIf(length(skills) > 0) as cnt_skills,
   219→        countIf(length(slash_commands) > 0) as cnt_slash
   220→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   221→      WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   222→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   223→        ${filters}
   224→    )
   225→    SELECT
   226→      cnt_sessions as total_sessions,
   227→      sum_interactions as total_interactions,
   228→      ifNull(round(avg_duration, 2), 0) as avg_session_duration_min,
   229→      ifNull(round(avg_interactions, 2), 0) as avg_interactions_per_session,
   230→      ifNull(round(avg_response, 2), 0) as avg_response_time_sec,
   231→      ifNull(round(med_response, 2), 0) as median_response_time_sec,
   232→      round(sum_quick_responses * 100.0 / if(sum_interactions > 0, sum_interactions, 1), 2) as quick_response_rate,
   233→      round(sum_long_pauses * 100.0 / if(sum_interactions > 0, sum_interactions, 1), 2) as long_pause_rate,
   234→      round(cnt_subagents * 100.0 / if(cnt_sessions > 0, cnt_sessions, 1), 2) as subagents_adoption_rate,
   235→      round(cnt_skills * 100.0 / if(cnt_sessions > 0, cnt_sessions, 1), 2) as skills_adoption_rate,
   236→      round(cnt_slash * 100.0 / if(cnt_sessions > 0, cnt_sessions, 1), 2) as slash_commands_adoption_rate
   237→    FROM totals
   238→  `;
   239→
   240→	const results = await queryClickhouse<SessionAnalyticsSummary>(query);
   241→
   242→	const defaults: SessionAnalyticsSummary = {
   243→		total_sessions: 0,
   244→		total_interactions: 0,
   245→		avg_session_duration_min: 0,
   246→		avg_interactions_per_session: 0,
   247→		avg_response_time_sec: 0,
   248→		median_response_time_sec: 0,
   249→		quick_response_rate: 0,
   250→		long_pause_rate: 0,
   251→		subagents_adoption_rate: 0,
   252→		skills_adoption_rate: 0,
   253→		slash_commands_adoption_rate: 0,
   254→	};
   255→
   256→	if (results.length === 0) {
   257→		return defaults;
   258→	}
   259→
   260→	// Coalesce nulls from ClickHouse (AVG on 0 rows returns null despite ifNull)
   261→	const row = results[0] as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   262→	if (!row) return defaults;
   263→	return Object.fromEntries(
   264→		Object.entries(defaults).map(([key, def]) => [key, row[key] ?? def]),
   265→	) as SessionAnalyticsSummary;
   266→}
   267→
   268→export type SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod = SessionAnalyticsSummaryBase;
   269→
   270→/**
   271→ * Get session analytics summary with period-over-period comparison
   272→ */
   273→export async function getSessionAnalyticsSummaryComparison(
   274→	orgId: string,
   275→	params: {
   276→		days?: number;
   277→		user_id?: string;
   278→		project_path?: string;
   279→	} = {},
   280→) {
   281→	const { days = 7, user_id, project_path } = params;
   282→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   283→	const d = Number(days);
   284→	const previousDays = d * 2;
   285→
   286→	let filters = "";
   287→	if (user_id) {
   288→		filters += ` AND user_id = '${escapeString(user_id)}'`;
   289→	}
   290→	if (project_path) {
   291→		filters += ` AND project_path = '${escapeString(project_path)}'`;
   292→	}
   293→
   294→	const summarySQL = (dateCondition: string) => `
   295→    WITH totals AS (
   296→      SELECT
   297→        COUNT(*) as cnt_sessions,
   298→        ifNull(AVG(actual_duration_min), 0) as avg_duration,
   299→        ifNull(AVG(avg_period_sec), 0) as avg_response,
   300→        countIf(length(subagent_types) > 0) as cnt_subagents,
   301→        countIf(length(skills) > 0) as cnt_skills,
   302→        countIf(length(slash_commands) > 0) as cnt_slash
   303→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   304→      WHERE ${dateCondition}
   305→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   306→        ${filters}
   307→    )
   308→    SELECT
   309→      cnt_sessions as total_sessions,
   310→      ifNull(round(avg_duration, 2), 0) as avg_session_duration_min,
   311→      ifNull(round(avg_response, 2), 0) as avg_response_time_sec,
   312→      round(cnt_subagents * 100.0 / if(cnt_sessions > 0, cnt_sessions, 1), 2) as subagents_adoption_rate,
   313→      round(cnt_skills * 100.0 / if(cnt_sessions > 0, cnt_sessions, 1), 2) as skills_adoption_rate,
   314→      round(cnt_slash * 100.0 / if(cnt_sessions > 0, cnt_sessions, 1), 2) as slash_commands_adoption_rate
   315→    FROM totals
   316→  `;
   317→
   318→	const currentQuery = summarySQL(buildDateFilter(d));
   319→	const previousQuery = summarySQL(
   320→		`session_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${previousDays} DAY AND session_date < now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY`,
   321→	);
   322→
   323→	const [currentData, previousData] = await Promise.all([
   324→		queryClickhouse<SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod>(currentQuery),
   325→		queryClickhouse<SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod>(previousQuery),
   326→	]);
   327→
   328→	const defaultPeriod: SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod = {
   329→		total_sessions: 0,
   330→		avg_session_duration_min: 0,
   331→		avg_response_time_sec: 0,
   332→		subagents_adoption_rate: 0,
   333→		skills_adoption_rate: 0,
   334→		slash_commands_adoption_rate: 0,
   335→	};
   336→
   337→	// Coalesce nulls from ClickHouse (AVG on 0 rows returns null despite ifNull)
   338→	const coalesce = (
   339→		row: SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod | undefined,
   340→	): SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod => {
   341→		if (!row) return { ...defaultPeriod };
   342→		return Object.fromEntries(
   343→			Object.entries(defaultPeriod).map(([key, def]) => [
   344→				key,
   345→				(row as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)[key] ?? def,
   346→			]),
   347→		) as SessionSummaryComparisonPeriod;
   348→	};
   349→	const current = coalesce(currentData[0]);
   350→	const previous = coalesce(previousData[0]);
   351→
   352→	const calculateChange = (curr: number, prev: number) => {
   353→		if (!prev || prev === 0) return 0;
   354→		return ((curr - prev) / prev) * 100;
   355→	};
   356→
   357→	const changes = {
   358→		total_sessions: calculateChange(
   359→			current.total_sessions || 0,
   360→			previous.total_sessions || 0,
   361→		),
   362→		avg_session_duration_min: calculateChange(
   363→			current.avg_session_duration_min || 0,
   364→			previous.avg_session_duration_min || 0,
   365→		),
   366→		avg_response_time_sec: calculateChange(
   367→			current.avg_response_time_sec || 0,
   368→			previous.avg_response_time_sec || 0,
   369→		),
   370→	};
   371→
   372→	return { current, previous, changes };
   373→}

   375→/**
   376→ * Get interaction timing distribution
   377→ */
   378→export async function getInteractionTimingDistribution(
   379→	orgId: string,
   380→	params: {
   381→		days?: number;
   382→		user_id?: string;
   383→		project_path?: string;
   384→	} = {},
   385→): Promise<Array<{ bucket: string; count: number; percentage: number }>> {
   386→	const { days = 30, user_id, project_path } = params;
   387→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   388→	const d = Number(days);
   389→
   390→	let filters = "";
   391→	if (user_id) {
   392→		filters += ` AND user_id = '${escapeString(user_id)}'`;
   393→	}
   394→	if (project_path) {
   395→		filters += ` AND project_path = '${escapeString(project_path)}'`;
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	const query = `
   399→    WITH total AS (
   400→      SELECT SUM(total_interactions) as total_count
   401→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   402→      WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   403→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   404→        ${filters}
   405→    )
   406→    SELECT
   407→      'Instant (< 5s)' as bucket,
   408→      SUM(quick_responses) as count,
   409→      round(SUM(quick_responses) * 100.0 / (SELECT total_count FROM total), 2) as percentage
   410→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   411→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   412→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   413→      ${filters}
   414→
   415→    UNION ALL
   416→
   417→    SELECT
   418→      'Normal (5-60s)' as bucket,
   419→      SUM(normal_responses) as count,
   420→      round(SUM(normal_responses) * 100.0 / (SELECT total_count FROM total), 2) as percentage
   421→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   422→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   423→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   424→      ${filters}
   425→
   426→    UNION ALL
   427→
   428→    SELECT
   429→      'Long Pause (> 5m)' as bucket,
   430→      SUM(long_pauses) as count,
   431→      round(SUM(long_pauses) * 100.0 / (SELECT total_count FROM total), 2) as percentage
   432→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   433→    WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   434→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   435→      ${filters}
   436→
   437→    ORDER BY count DESC
   438→  `;
   439→
   440→	return queryClickhouse<{ bucket: string; count: number; percentage: number }>(
   441→		query,
   442→	);
   443→}

   445→/**
   446→ * Get flexible dimension analysis with optional split-by for stacked charts
   447→ */
   448→
   449→// Map metric to SQL expression
   450→const METRIC_EXPRESSIONS: Record<DimensionAnalysisInput["metric"], string> = {
   451→	session_count: "COUNT(*)",
   452→	avg_duration: "round(AVG(actual_duration_min), 2)",
   453→	total_duration: "round(SUM(actual_duration_min) / 60, 2)",
   454→	avg_interactions: "round(AVG(total_interactions), 2)",
   455→	total_interactions: "SUM(total_interactions)",
   456→	avg_response_time: "round(AVG(avg_period_sec), 2)",
   457→	median_response_time: "round(AVG(median_period_sec), 2)",
   458→	avg_tokens: "round(AVG(total_tokens), 0)",
   459→	total_tokens: "SUM(total_tokens)",
   460→	avg_success_score: "round(AVG(success_score), 2)",
   461→	avg_errors: "round(AVG(error_count), 2)",
   462→	total_errors: "SUM(error_count)",
   463→};
   464→
   465→export async function getSessionDimensionAnalysis(
   466→	orgId: string,
   467→	params: {
   468→		days?: number;
   469→		dimension: DimensionAnalysisInput["dimension"];
   470→		metric: DimensionAnalysisInput["metric"];
   471→		split_by?: DimensionAnalysisInput["dimension"];
   472→		limit?: number;
   473→		user_id?: string;
   474→		project_path?: string;
   475→	},
   476→) {
   477→	const {
   478→		days = 7,
   479→		dimension,
   480→		metric,
   481→		split_by,
   482→		limit = 10,
   483→		user_id,
   484→		project_path,
   485→	} = params;
   486→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   487→	const d = Number(days);
   488→	const lim = Number(limit);
   489→
   490→	const metricExpression = METRIC_EXPRESSIONS[metric];
   491→
   492→	// Map dimension to SQL expression (for computed dimensions)
   493→	const dimensionExpressions: Record<string, string> = {
   494→		used_skills: "if(length(skills) > 0, 1, 0)",
   495→		used_slash_commands: "if(length(slash_commands) > 0, 1, 0)",
   496→		used_subagents: "if(length(subagent_types) > 0, 1, 0)",
   497→	};
   498→
   499→	const dimensionExpression = dimensionExpressions[dimension] || dimension;
   500→	const splitByExpression = split_by
   501→		? dimensionExpressions[split_by] || split_by
   502→		: null;
   503→
   504→	let filters = "";
   505→	if (user_id) {
   506→		filters += ` AND user_id = '${escapeString(user_id)}'`;
   507→	}
   508→	if (project_path) {
   509→		filters += ` AND project_path = '${escapeString(project_path)}'`;
   510→	}
   511→
   512→	let query: string;
   513→
   514→	if (split_by) {
   515→		query = `
   516→      SELECT
   517→        ${dimensionExpression} as dimension_value,
   518→        ${splitByExpression} as split_value,
   519→        ${metricExpression} as metric_value
   520→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   521→      WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   522→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   523→        ${filters}
   524→      GROUP BY dimension_value, split_value
   525→      ORDER BY metric_value DESC
   526→    `;
   527→	} else {
   528→		query = `
   529→      SELECT
   530→        ${dimensionExpression} as dimension_value,
   531→        ${metricExpression} as metric_value
   532→      FROM rudel.session_analytics
   533→      WHERE ${buildDateFilter(d)}
   534→        AND organization_id = '${org}'
   535→        ${filters}
   536→      GROUP BY dimension_value
   537→      ORDER BY metric_value DESC
   538→      LIMIT ${lim}
   539→    `;
   540→	}
   541→
   542→	interface DimensionRow {
   543→		dimension_value: string;
   544→		split_value?: string;
   545→		metric_value: number;
   546→	}
   547→
   548→	const results = await queryClickhouse<DimensionRow>(query);
   549→
   550→	if (split_by) {
   551→		const grouped = new Map<string, Record<string, number>>();
   552→		const totalMetric = new Map<string, number>();
   553→
   554→		for (const row of results) {
   555→			const dimVal = String(row.dimension_value);
   556→			const splitVal = String(row.split_value);
   557→			const metricVal = Number(row.metric_value);
   558→
   559→			if (!grouped.has(dimVal)) {
   560→				grouped.set(dimVal, {});
   561→				totalMetric.set(dimVal, 0);
   562→			}
   563→
   564→			const group = grouped.get(dimVal);
   565→			if (group) group[splitVal] = metricVal;
   566→			totalMetric.set(dimVal, (totalMetric.get(dimVal) || 0) + metricVal);
   567→		}
   568→
   569→		const finalData = Array.from(grouped.entries())
   570→			.map(([dimension_value, split_values]) => ({
   571→				dimension_value,
   572→				split_values,
   573→				_total: totalMetric.get(dimension_value) || 0,
   574→			}))
   575→			.sort((a, b) => b._total - a._total)
   576→			.slice(0, lim)
   577→			.map(({ dimension_value, split_values }) => ({
   578→				dimension_value,
   579→				split_values,
   580→			}));
   581→
   582→		return finalData;
   583→	}
   584→
   585→	return results.map((row) => ({
   586→		dimension_value: String(row.dimension_value),
   587→		metric_value: Number(row.metric_value),
   588→	}));
   589→}

    15→/**
    16→ * Get learnings feed from compound:feedback sessions
    17→ */
    18→export async function getLearningsFeed(
    19→	orgId: string,
    20→	params: {
    21→		days?: number;
    22→		user_id?: string;
    23→		project_path?: string;
    24→		limit?: number;
    25→		offset?: number;
    26→	} = {},
    27→): Promise<LearningEntry[]> {
    28→	const { days = 30, user_id, project_path, limit = 50, offset = 0 } = params;
    29→
    30→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
    31→	const d = Number(days);
    32→	const lim = Number(limit);
    33→	const off = Number(offset);
    34→
    35→	let filters = `last_interaction_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY
    36→    AND has(slash_commands, 'compound:feedback')
    37→    AND organization_id = '${org}'`;
    38→
    39→	if (user_id) {
    40→		filters += `\n    AND user_id = '${escapeString(user_id)}'`;
    41→	}
    42→	if (project_path) {
    43→		filters += `\n    AND project_path = '${escapeString(project_path)}'`;
    44→	}
    45→
    46→	const query = `
    47→    WITH
    48→      feedback_data AS (
    49→        SELECT
    50→          session_id,
    51→          user_id,
    52→          last_interaction_date,
    53→          project_path,
    54→          organization_id,
    55→          repository,
    56→          subagents,
    57→          skills,
    58→          slash_commands,
    59→          content
    60→        FROM rudel.session_analytics
    61→        WHERE ${filters}
    62→      )
    63→    SELECT
    64→      session_id,
    65→      user_id,
    66→      formatDateTime(last_interaction_date, '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%i:%SZ') as created_at,
    67→      'feedback' as type,
    68→      if(
    69→        position(content, '<command-name>/compound:feedback</command-name>') > 0
    70→        AND position(content, '<command-args>', position(content, '<command-name>/compound:feedback</command-name>')) > 0
    71→        AND position(content, '</command-args>', position(content, '<command-name>/compound:feedback</command-name>')) > 0,
    72→        trimBoth(
    73→          substring(
    74→            content,
    75→            position(content, '<command-args>', position(content, '<command-name>/compound:feedback</command-name>')) + 14,
    76→            least(
    77→              position(content, '</command-args>', position(content, '<command-name>/compound:feedback</command-name>'))
    78→              - position(content, '<command-args>', position(content, '<command-name>/compound:feedback</command-name>'))
    79→              - 14,
    80→              5000
    81→            )
    82→          )
    83→        ),
    84→        concat('Feedback session in ', if(repository != '', repository, project_path))
    85→      ) as content,
    86→      'project' as scope,
    87→      [] as tags,
    88→      project_path,
    89→      organization_id,
    90→      repository,
    91→      mapKeys(subagents) as subagents,
    92→      skills,
    93→      arrayFilter(x -> x != 'compound:feedback', slash_commands) as other_commands
    94→    FROM feedback_data
    95→    ORDER BY last_interaction_date DESC
    96→    LIMIT ${lim}
    97→    OFFSET ${off}
    98→  `;
    99→
   100→	return queryClickhouse<LearningEntry>(query);
   101→}

   207→/**
   208→ * Get learnings trend over time with splits by user_id or repository
   209→ */
   210→export async function getLearningsTrend(
   211→	orgId: string,
   212→	params: {
   213→		days?: number;
   214→		split_by: "user_id" | "repository";
   215→	},
   216→): Promise<LearningsTrendDataPoint[]> {
   217→	const { days = 30, split_by } = params;
   218→	const org = escapeString(orgId);
   219→	const d = Number(days);
   220→
   221→	const splitColumn =
   222→		split_by === "repository"
   223→			? "if(repository != '', repository, 'Unknown')"
   224→			: "user_id";
   225→
   226→	const query = `
   227→    SELECT
   228→      toDate(last_interaction_date) as date,
   229→      ${splitColumn} as split_key,
   230→      count() as count
   231→    FROM rudel.session_analytics
   232→    WHERE last_interaction_date >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${d} DAY
   233→      AND has(slash_commands, 'compound:feedback')
   234→      AND organization_id = '${org}'
   235→    GROUP BY date, split_key
   236→    ORDER BY date ASC, split_key ASC
   237→  `;
   238→
   239→	const rawData = await queryClickhouse<{
   240→		date: string;
   241→		split_key: string;
   242→		count: number;
   243→	}>(query);
   244→
   245→	// Transform data into chart-friendly format
   246→	const dataByDate = new Map<string, Record<string, number>>();
   247→	const allSplitKeys = new Set<string>();
   248→
   249→	rawData.forEach((row) => {
   250→		if (!dataByDate.has(row.date)) {
   251→			dataByDate.set(row.date, {});
   252→		}
   253→		const entry = dataByDate.get(row.date);
   254→		if (entry) entry[row.split_key] = row.count;
   255→		allSplitKeys.add(row.split_key);
   256→	});
   257→
   258→	const chartData: LearningsTrendDataPoint[] = Array.from(dataByDate.entries())
   259→		.map(([date, counts]) => {
   260→			const dataPoint: LearningsTrendDataPoint = { date };
   261→			allSplitKeys.forEach((key) => {
   262→				dataPoint[key] = counts[key] || 0;
   263→			});
   264→			return dataPoint;
   265→		})
   266→		.sort((a, b) => (a.date as string).localeCompare(b.date as string));
   267→
   268→	return chartData;

{"parentUuid":"89421655-c801-470f-bd6c-5e641a4e3813","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"REDACTED","toolUseID":"REDACTED","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:34.037Z","uuid":"2eedfaca-3fdd-4e5a-8531-9f2842ed3d3a"}
{"parentUuid":"89421655-c801-470f-bd6c-5e641a4e3813","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { implement, ORPCError } from "@orpc/server";
     2→import { contract } from "@rudel/api-routes";
     3→import type { Session } from "./auth.js";
     4→
     5→export interface AppContext {
     6→	user: Session["user"] | null;
     7→	session: Session["session"] | null;
     8→}
     9→
    10→export const os = implement(contract).$context<AppContext>();
    11→
    12→export const authMiddleware = os.middleware(async ({ context, next }) => {
    13→	if (!context.user || !context.session) {
    14→		throw new ORPCError("UNAUTHORIZED");
    15→	}
    16→	return next({
    17→		context: {
    18→			user: context.user,
    19→			session: context.session,
    20→		},
    21→	});
    22→});
    23→
    24→export const orgMiddleware = os.middleware(async ({ context, next }) => {
    25→	if (!context.user || !context.session) {
    26→		throw new ORPCError("UNAUTHORIZED");
    27→	}
    28→	const organizationId =
    29→		(context.session as Record<string, unknown>).activeOrganizationId ??
    30→		context.user.id;
    31→	if (!organizationId || typeof organizationId !== "string") {
    32→		throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
    33→			message: "No active organization",
    34→		});
    35→	}
    36→	return next({
    37→		context: {
    38→			user: context.user,
    39→			session: context.session,
    40→			organizationId,
    41→		},
    42→	});
    43→});
    44→"}]},"uuid":"5cbaf1a4-ac75-4803-9cef-27bf4eed3c94","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:34.037Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"89421655-c801-470f-bd6c-5e641a4e3813"}

{"parentUuid":"3fc7736e-2ce1-4df4-9922-b77a90682087","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_01UBBEjCxtzHVz3FGWf3u91s","toolUseID":"toolu_01UBBEjCxtzHVz3FGWf3u91s","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:34.468Z","uuid":"469eb8d4-3a1e-46ad-9198-c204b3a7dcbc"}
{"parentUuid":"3fc7736e-2ce1-4df4-9922-b77a90682087","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UBBEjCxtzHVz3FGWf3u91s","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { createClient } from "@clickhouse/client-web";
     2→
     3→export interface ClickHouseExecutor {
     4→	execute(sql: string): Promise<void>;
     5→	query<T>(sql: string): Promise<T[]>;
     6→	insert(params: { table: string; values: object[] }): Promise<void>;
     7→}
     8→
     9→export function createClickHouseExecutor(config: {
    10→	url: string;
    11→	username?: string;
    12→	password?: string;
    13→	database?: string;
    14→}): ClickHouseExecutor {
    15→	const client = createClient({
    16→		url: config.url,
    17→		username: config.username,
    18→		password: config.password,
    19→		database: config.database,
    20→		clickhouse_settings: {
    21→			wait_end_of_query: 1,
    22→		},
    23→	});
    24→	return {
    25→		async execute(sql) {
    26→			await client.command({ query: sql });
    27→		},
    28→		async query<T>(sql: string): Promise<T[]> {
    29→			const result = await client.query({
    30→				query: sql,
    31→				format: "JSONEachRow",
    32→			});
    33→			return result.json();
    34→		},
    35→		async insert(params) {
    36→			// Use command() with FORMAT JSONEachRow instead of client.insert()
    37→			// because ClickHouse Cloud's @clickhouse/client insert() silently drops data.
    38→			const rows = params.values.map((r) => JSON.stringify(r)).join("\n");
    39→			await client.command({
    40→				query: `INSERT INTO ${params.table} SETTINGS async_insert=0 FORMAT JSONEachRow ${rows}`,
    41→			});
    42→		},
    43→	};
    44→}
    45→
    46→let _clickhouse: ClickHouseExecutor | null = null;
    47→
    48→export function getClickhouse(): ClickHouseExecutor {
    49→	if (!_clickhouse) {
    50→		const executor = createClickHouseExecutor({
    51→			url: process.env.CLICKHOUSE_URL || "http://localhost:8123",
    52→			username:
    53→				process.env.CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME ||
    54→				process.env.CLICKHOUSE_USER ||
    55→				"default",
    56→			password: process.env.CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD || "",
    57→			database: "default",
    58→		});
    59→		const maxRetries = 3;
    60→		_clickhouse = {
    61→			...executor,
    62→			async insert(params) {
    63→				for (let attempt = 0; attempt < maxRetries; attempt++) {
    64→					try {
    65→						return await executor.insert(params);
    66→					} catch (error) {
    67→						// Retry on INSERT race conditions (ClickHouse code 236)
    68→						if (attempt === maxRetries - 1) throw error;
    69→						await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 100 * (attempt + 1)));
    70→					}
    71→				}
    72→			},
    73→		};
    74→	}
    75→	return _clickhouse;
    76→}
    77→
    78→export function escapeString(value: string): string {
    79→	return value.replace(/'/g, "\\'");
    80→}
    81→
    82→export function buildDateFilter(days: number, column = "session_date"): string {
    83→	return `${column} >= now64(3) - INTERVAL ${Number(days)} DAY AND ${column} <= now64(3)`;
    84→}
    85→
    86→export async function queryClickhouse<T>(sql: string): Promise<T[]> {
    87→	const ch = getClickhouse();
    88→	return ch.query<T>(sql);
    89→}
    90→"}]},"uuid":"478b8ff6-e0b3-451d-b237-925ea08fd104","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:34.468Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3fc7736e-2ce1-4df4-9922-b77a90682087"}

{"parentUuid":"c54d2679-2fdb-417e-af45-31058329dc60","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"toolu_01ToHZJbE9DjHfpj4f4fmpac","toolUseID":"toolu_01ToHZJbE9DjHfpj4f4fmpac","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:35.025Z","uuid":"4499b95f-a796-4ff7-bd82-b79234c16b58"}
{"parentUuid":"c54d2679-2fdb-417e-af45-31058329dc60","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ToHZJbE9DjHfpj4f4fmpac","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Self-Hosting Rudel
     2→
     3→This guide walks through deploying Rudel using **ObsessionDB** (ClickHouse), **Neon** (Postgres), and **Fly.io** (app server). This is the same stack we use in production.
     4→
     5→> **This is an opinionated guide.** Rudel is a Bun server that needs Postgres and ClickHouse — it can run anywhere those are available. You don't have to use these specific providers. Any Postgres instance, any ClickHouse instance (that supports `SharedReplacingMergeTree` or `ReplacingMergeTree`), and any platform that can run a Bun/Node.js server will work. We use ObsessionDB, Neon, and Fly.io because they all have free tiers and are easy to set up.
     6→
     7→## Services
     8→
     9→| Component | Provider | Free Tier | Purpose |
    10→|-----------|----------|-----------|---------|
    11→| **ClickHouse** | [ObsessionDB](https://obsessiondb.com) | Yes | Session transcript storage and analytics |
    12→| **Postgres** | [Neon](https://neon.tech) | Yes (0.5 GB) | Authentication (users, sessions, accounts) |
    13→| **App Server** | [Fly.io](https://fly.io) | Yes (3 shared VMs) | HTTP API + static frontend serving |
    14→
    15→## 1. Provision ClickHouse (ObsessionDB)
    16→
    17→1. Create an account at [obsessiondb.com](https://obsessiondb.com)
    18→2. Create a new ClickHouse instance
    19→3. Note your connection details:
    20→   - **Host** — the HTTPS endpoint (e.g. `https://your-instance.obsessiondb.com`)
    21→   - **Username** — your username
    22→   - **Password** — your password
    23→4. Apply the schema migration:
    24→
    25→```bash
    26→# From the repo root — set the env vars that chkit reads
    27→CLICKHOUSE_URL=https://your-instance.obsessiondb.com \
    28→CLICKHOUSE_USER=your-username \
    29→REDACTED \
    30→CLICKHOUSE_DB=default \
    31→  bun --bun --cwd packages/ch-schema chkit migrate --apply
    32→```
    33→
    34→The migration creates the `rudel` database, the `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics` tables, and a materialized view that computes analytics on insert. The cloud migration uses `SharedReplacingMergeTree` for `claude_sessions`.
    35→
    36→> **Note**: `CLICKHOUSE_DB` must be set to `default` for the migration because the `rudel` database doesn't exist yet — the migration creates it. `CLICKHOUSE_USER` (not `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME`) is the env var name that the migration tool expects.
    37→
    38→## 2. Provision Postgres (Neon)
    39→
    40→1. Create an account at [neon.tech](https://neon.tech)
    41→2. Create a new project and database named `rudel`
    42→3. Copy your connection string (looks like `postgres://user:pass@host/rudel?sslmode=require`)
    43→4. Run the Drizzle migrations:
    44→
    45→```bash
    46→PG_CONNECTION_STRING="postgres://user:pass@host/rudel?sslmode=require" \
    47→  bun run --cwd packages/sql-schema migrate
    48→```
    49→
    50→This creates the auth tables (users, sessions, accounts, verification tokens) used by `better-auth`.
    51→
    52→## 3. Deploy to Fly.io
    53→
    54→1. Install the Fly CLI: `curl -L https://fly.io/install.sh | sh`
    55→2. Sign up or log in: `fly auth login`
    56→3. Create a new app:
    57→
    58→```bash
    59→fly launch --name your-app-name --no-deploy
    60→```
    61→
    62→4. Set secrets (the API reads `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME`, not `CLICKHOUSE_USER`):
    63→
    64→```bash
    65→fly secrets set \
    66→  PG_CONNECTION_STRING="postgres://user:pass@host/rudel?sslmode=require" \
    67→  BETTER_AUTH_SECRET="$(openssl rand -base64 32)" \
    68→  CLICKHOUSE_URL="https://your-instance.obsessiondb.com" \
    69→  CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME="your-username" \
    70→  CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD="your-password" \
    71→  APP_URL="https://your-app-name.fly.dev" \
    72→  ALLOWED_ORIGIN="https://your-app-name.fly.dev"
    73→```
    74→
    75→5. Deploy (the `Dockerfile` in the repo root handles building the frontend and running the API):
    76→
    77→```bash
    78→fly deploy
    79→```
    80→
    81→6. Verify it's running:
    82→
    83→```bash
    84→curl https://your-app-name.fly.dev/health
    85→```
    86→
    87→### Custom domain
    88→
    89→If you want to use a custom domain instead of `*.fly.dev`:
    90→
    91→```bash
    92→fly certs add your-domain.com
    93→```
    94→
    95→Then update `APP_URL` and `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` to match:
    96→
    97→```bash
    98→fly secrets set \
    99→  APP_URL="https://your-domain.com" \
   100→  ALLOWED_ORIGIN="https://your-domain.com"
   101→```
   102→
   103→## Social Login (Optional)
   104→
   105→To enable GitHub or Google OAuth login, create OAuth apps with the respective providers and set the client credentials:
   106→
   107→```bash
   108→fly secrets set \
   109→  GITHUB_CLIENT_ID="your-client-id" \
   110→  GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET="your-client-secret"
   111→```
   112→
   113→Set the OAuth callback URL to `https://your-domain.com/api/auth/callback/github` (or `/google`).
   114→
   115→Without these, users can still sign up and log in with email/password.
   116→
   117→## Environment Variables Reference
   118→
   119→| Variable | Required | Description |
   120→|----------|----------|-------------|
   121→| `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` | Yes | Postgres connection string |
   122→| `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` | Yes | Auth secret (`openssl rand -base64 32`) |
   123→| `CLICKHOUSE_URL` | Yes | ClickHouse HTTPS endpoint |
   124→| `CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME` | Yes | ClickHouse username |
   125→| `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD` | Yes | ClickHouse password |
   126→| `APP_URL` | Yes | Public URL of the deployed app |
   127→| `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` | Yes | CORS origin (same as `APP_URL` for single-domain) |

     2→
     3→export const user = pgTable("user", {

    17→
    18→export const session = pgTable("session", {

    24→	token: text("token").notNull().unique(),

    31→		.notNull(),
    32→	ipAddress: text("ip_address"),
    33→	userAgent: text("user_agent"),
    34→	userId: text("user_id")
    35→		.notNull()
    36→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    37→	activeOrganizationId: text("active_organization_id"),
    38→});

    40→export const account = pgTable("account", {
    41→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),

    43→	providerId: text("provider_id").notNull(),

    47→	accessToken: text("access_token"),

    86→export const organization = pgTable("organization", {

{"parentUuid":"e3f295a0-bada-482a-bd44-389b6d63db75","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { Building2, Check, Loader2, X } from "lucide-react";
     2→import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
     3→import { useNavigate, useParams } from "react-router-dom";
     4→import { Button } from "../components/ui/button";
     5→import { authClient } from "../lib/auth-client";
     6→
     7→export function AcceptInvitationPage() {
     8→	const { invitationId } = useParams<{ invitationId: string }>();
     9→	const navigate = useNavigate();
    10→	const { data: session, isPending: sessionLoading } = authClient.useSession();
    11→	const [status, setStatus] = useState<
    12→		"loading" | "accepting" | "accepted" | "error"
    13→	>("loading");
    14→	const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    15→
    16→	useEffect(() => {
    17→		if (sessionLoading || !invitationId) return;
    18→
    19→		if (!session) {
    20→			const returnUrl = `/invitation/${invitationId}`;
    21→			window.location.href = `/?redirect=${encodeURIComponent(returnUrl)}`;
    22→			return;
    23→		}
    24→
    25→		setStatus("loading");
    26→	}, [session, sessionLoading, invitationId]);
    27→
    28→	const handleAccept = async () => {
    29→		if (!invitationId) return;
    30→		setStatus("accepting");
    31→		setError(null);
    32→
    33→		const res = await authClient.organization.acceptInvitation({
    34→			invitationId,
    35→		});
    36→
    37→		if (res.error) {
    38→			setError(res.error.message ?? "Failed to accept invitation");
    39→			setStatus("error");
    40→			return;
    41→		}
    42→
    43→		if (res.data) {
    44→			await authClient.organization.setActive({
    45→				organizationId: res.data.member.organizationId,
    46→			});
    47→		}
    48→
    49→		setStatus("accepted");
    50→		setTimeout(() => navigate("/dashboard"), 1500);
    51→	};
    52→
    53→	const handleReject = async () => {
    54→		if (!invitationId) return;
    55→		await authClient.organization.rejectInvitation({ invitationId });
    56→		navigate("/dashboard");
    57→	};
    58→
    59→	if (sessionLoading) {
    60→		return (
    61→			<div className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center">
    62→				<Loader2 className="h-6 w-6 animate-spin text-muted" />
    63→			</div>
    64→		);
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	return (
    68→		<div className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center">
    69→			<div className="w-full max-w-sm rounded-xl border border-border bg-surface p-8 text-center">
    70→				<div className="mx-auto mb-4 flex h-12 w-12 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-hover">
    71→					{status === "accepted" ? (
    72→						<Check className="h-6 w-6 text-status-success-icon" />
    73→					) : (
    74→						<Building2 className="h-6 w-6 text-accent" />
    75→					)}
    76→				</div>
    77→
    78→				{status === "accepted" ? (
    79→					<>
    80→						<h1 className="text-lg font-semibold text-heading mb-2">
    81→							Welcome to the team!
    82→						</h1>
    83→						<p className="text-sm text-muted">Redirecting to dashboard...</p>
    84→					</>
    85→				) : (
    86→					<>
    87→						<h1 className="text-lg font-semibold text-heading mb-2">
    88→							You've been invited
    89→						</h1>
    90→						<p className="text-sm text-muted mb-6">
    91→							Accept this invitation to join the organization.
    92→						</p>
    93→
    94→						{error && <p className="text-sm text-red-500 mb-4">{error}</p>}
    95→
    96→						<div className="flex gap-3 justify-center">
    97→							<Button
    98→								variant="outline"
    99→								size="sm"
   100→								onClick={handleReject}
   101→								disabled={status === "accepting"}
   102→							>
   103→								<X className="h-4 w-4 mr-1" />
   104→								Decline
   105→							</Button>
   106→							<Button
   107→								size="sm"
   108→								onClick={handleAccept}
   109→								disabled={status === "accepting"}
   110→							>
   111→								{status === "accepting" ? (
   112→									<Loader2 className="h-4 w-4 mr-1 animate-spin" />
   113→								) : (
   114→									<Check className="h-4 w-4 mr-1" />
   115→								)}
   116→								Accept
   117→							</Button>
   118→						</div>
   119→					</>
   120→				)}
   121→			</div>
   122→		</div>
   123→	);
   124→}
   125→"}]},"uuid":"a2bf5d5f-e1c2-4ac2-8429-07afeb46b846","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:41.042Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e3f295a0-bada-482a-bd44-389b6d63db75"}

{"parentUuid":"28171208-864b-4846-82f1-8fe60ee6b73d","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"progress","data":{"type":"hook_progress","hookEvent":"PostToolUse","hookName":"PostToolUse:Read","command":"callback"},"parentToolUseID":"REDACTED","toolUseID":"REDACTED","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:41.692Z","uuid":"41aa96d2-0aa6-4235-9234-b8c54fb8eeb1"}
{"parentUuid":"28171208-864b-4846-82f1-8fe60ee6b73d","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { useQueryClient } from "@tanstack/react-query";
     2→import {
     3→	createContext,
     4→	type ReactNode,
     5→	useContext,
     6→	useEffect,
     7→	useState,
     8→} from "react";
     9→import { authClient } from "../lib/auth-client";
    10→
    11→interface Organization {
    12→	id: string;
    13→	name: string;
    14→	slug: string;
    15→	logo?: string | null | undefined;
    16→}
    17→
    18→interface OrganizationContextType {
    19→	activeOrg: Organization | null;
    20→	organizations: readonly Organization[];
    21→	switchOrg: (orgId: string) => Promise<void>;
    22→	isLoading: boolean;
    23→}
    24→
    25→const OrganizationContext = createContext<OrganizationContextType | undefined>(
    26→	undefined,
    27→);
    28→
    29→export function OrganizationProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
    30→	const { data: activeOrg, isPending: activeLoading } =
    31→		authClient.useActiveOrganization();
    32→	const { data: orgs, isPending: listLoading } =
    33→		authClient.useListOrganizations();
    34→	const queryClient = useQueryClient();
    35→	const [switching, setSwitching] = useState(false);
    36→
    37→	// Auto-set active org if none is set but user has orgs
    38→	useEffect(() => {
    39→		if (
    40→			!activeLoading &&
    41→			!listLoading &&
    42→			!activeOrg &&
    43→			orgs &&
    44→			orgs.length > 0 &&
    45→			!switching
    46→		) {
    47→			setSwitching(true);
    48→			authClient.organization
    49→				.setActive({ organizationId: orgs[0].id })
    50→				.then(() => queryClient.invalidateQueries())
    51→				.finally(() => setSwitching(false));
    52→		}
    53→	}, [activeOrg, orgs, activeLoading, listLoading, switching, queryClient]);
    54→
    55→	const switchOrg = async (orgId: string) => {
    56→		setSwitching(true);
    57→		try {
    58→			await authClient.organization.setActive({ organizationId: orgId });
    59→			await queryClient.invalidateQueries();
    60→		} finally {
    61→			setSwitching(false);
    62→		}
    63→	};
    64→
    65→	return (
    66→		<OrganizationContext.Provider
    67→			value={{
    68→				activeOrg: activeOrg ?? null,
    69→				organizations: orgs ?? [],
    70→				switchOrg,
    71→				isLoading: activeLoading || listLoading || switching,
    72→			}}
    73→		>
    74→			{children}
    75→		</OrganizationContext.Provider>
    76→	);
    77→}
    78→
    79→export function useOrganization() {
    80→	const context = useContext(OrganizationContext);
    81→	if (context === undefined) {
    82→		throw new Error(
    83→			"useOrganization must be used within an OrganizationProvider",
    84→		);
    85→	}
    86→	return context;
    87→}
    88→"}]},"uuid":"94dfa812-69a7-40df-a858-b463a16789dc","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:41.691Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"28171208-864b-4846-82f1-8fe60ee6b73d"}

{"parentUuid":"dbea10fa-4a2e-4a61-9bec-cba237b5341f","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import {
     2→	Building2,
     3→	Check,
     4→	Copy,
     5→	Loader2,
     6→	Mail,
     7→	Plus,
     8→	Trash2,
     9→	UserPlus,
    10→	X,
    11→} from "lucide-react";
    12→import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
    13→import { Link } from "react-router-dom";
    14→import { AnalyticsCard } from "../../components/analytics/AnalyticsCard";
    15→import { PageHeader } from "../../components/analytics/PageHeader";
    16→import { Badge } from "../../components/ui/badge";
    17→import { Button } from "../../components/ui/button";
    18→import { Input } from "../../components/ui/input";
    19→import { useOrganization } from "../../contexts/OrganizationContext";
    20→import { authClient } from "../../lib/auth-client";
    21→
    22→interface FullOrg {
    23→	id: string;
    24→	name: string;
    25→	slug: string;
    26→	members: readonly {
    27→		id: string;
    28→		userId: string;
    29→		role: string;
    30→		user: { id: string; name: string; email: string; image: string | null };
    31→	}[];
    32→	invitations: readonly {
    33→		id: string;
    34→		email: string;
    35→		role: string | null;
    36→		status: string;
    37→	}[];
    38→}
    39→
    40→export function OrganizationPage() {
    41→	const { activeOrg } = useOrganization();
    42→	const [fullOrg, setFullOrg] = useState<FullOrg | null>(null);
    43→	const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    44→	const [inviteEmail, setInviteEmail] = useState("");
    45→	const [inviteRole, setInviteRole] = useState("member");
    46→	const [inviting, setInviting] = useState(false);
    47→	const [inviteLink, setInviteLink] = useState<string | null>(null);
    48→	const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
    49→
    50→	const fetchOrg = async () => {
    51→		if (!activeOrg) return;
    52→		setLoading(true);
    53→		const res = await authClient.organization.getFullOrganization({
    54→			query: { organizationId: activeOrg.id },
    55→		});
    56→		if (res.data) {
    57→			setFullOrg(res.data as unknown as FullOrg);
    58→		}
    59→		setLoading(false);
    60→	};
    61→
    62→	// biome-ignore lint/correctness/useExhaustiveDependencies: refetch when org changes
    63→	useEffect(() => {
    64→		fetchOrg();
    65→	}, [activeOrg?.id]);
    66→
    67→	const handleInvite = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    68→		e.preventDefault();
    69→		if (!inviteEmail.trim()) return;
    70→		setInviting(true);
    71→		const res = await authClient.organization.inviteMember({
    72→			email: inviteEmail.trim(),
    73→			role: inviteRole as "member" | "admin",
    74→		});
    75→		if (res.data) {
    76→			const link = `${window.location.origin}/invitation/${res.data.id}`;
    77→			setInviteLink(link);
    78→			setInviteEmail("");
    79→			await fetchOrg();
    80→		}
    81→		setInviting(false);
    82→	};
    83→
    84→	const handleCopyLink = () => {
    85→		if (!inviteLink) return;
    86→		navigator.clipboard.writeText(inviteLink);
    87→		setCopied(true);
    88→		setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000);
    89→	};
    90→
    91→	const handleRemoveMember = async (memberIdOrEmail: string) => {
    92→		await authClient.organization.removeMember({ memberIdOrEmail });
    93→		await fetchOrg();
    94→	};
    95→
    96→	const handleCancelInvitation = async (invitationId: string) => {
    97→		await authClient.organization.cancelInvitation({ invitationId });
    98→		await fetchOrg();
    99→	};
   100→
   101→	if (!activeOrg) {
   102→		return (
   103→			<div className="px-8 py-6">
   104→				<p className="text-muted">No organization selected.</p>
   105→			</div>
   106→		);
   107→	}
   108→
   109→	return (
   110→		<div className="px-8 py-6">
   111→			<PageHeader
   112→				title="Organization"
   113→				description={`Manage ${activeOrg.name}`}
   114→				actions={
   115→					<Link to="/dashboard/organization/new">
   116→						<Button size="sm">
   117→							<Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-1" />
   118→							Create Organization
   119→						</Button>
   120→					</Link>
   121→				}
   122→			/>
   123→
   124→			<div className="flex flex-col gap-6">
   125→				{/* Organization Info */}
   126→				<AnalyticsCard>
   127→					<div className="flex items-center gap-4">
   128→						<div className="flex h-12 w-12 items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-hover">
   129→							<Building2 className="h-6 w-6 text-muted" />
   130→						</div>
   131→						<div>
   132→							<h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-heading">
   133→								{activeOrg.name}
   134→							</h2>
   135→							<p className="text-sm text-muted">/{activeOrg.slug}</p>
   136→						</div>
   137→					</div>
   138→				</AnalyticsCard>
   139→
   140→				{/* Invite Members */}
   141→				<AnalyticsCard>
   142→					<h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-heading mb-4">
   143→						<UserPlus className="h-5 w-5 inline-block mr-2 -mt-0.5" />
   144→						Invite Members
   145→					</h2>
   146→
   147→					<form onSubmit={handleInvite} className="flex gap-2 mb-3">
   148→						<Input
   149→							type="email"
   150→							placeholder="Email address"
   151→							value={inviteEmail}
   152→							onChange={(e) => setInviteEmail(e.target.value)}
   153→							className="flex-1"
   154→						/>
   155→						<select
   156→							value={inviteRole}
   157→							onChange={(e) => setInviteRole(e.target.value)}
   158→							className="rounded-md border border-border bg-surface px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-foreground"
   159→						>
   160→							<option value="member">Member</option>
   161→							<option value="admin">Admin</option>
   162→						</select>
   163→						<Button
   164→							type="submit"
   165→							size="sm"
   166→							disabled={inviting || !inviteEmail.trim()}
   167→						>
   168→							{inviting ? (
   169→								<Loader2 className="h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />
   170→							) : (
   171→								<Mail className="h-4 w-4 mr-1" />
   172→							)}
   173→							Invite
   174→						</Button>
   175→					</form>
   176→
   177→					{inviteLink && (
   178→						<div className="flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-border bg-hover/50 px-3 py-2 text-sm">
   179→							<span className="flex-1 truncate text-muted">{inviteLink}</span>

{"parentUuid":"702afad9-e0f5-44c4-9530-c0ebc82ea4ad","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { createInterface } from "node:readline";
     2→import { buildCommand } from "@stricli/core";
     3→import { createApiClient } from "../lib/api-client.js";
     4→import {
     5→	addHook,
     6→	getClaudeSettingsPath,
     7→	isHookEnabled,
     8→} from "../lib/claude-settings.js";
     9→import { loadCredentials } from "../lib/credentials.js";
    10→import { getProjectOrgId, setProjectOrgId } from "../lib/project-config.js";
    11→
    12→async function promptChoice(question: string, max: number): Promise<number> {
    13→	const rl = createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
    14→	return new Promise((resolve) => {
    15→		rl.question(question, (answer) => {
    16→			rl.close();
    17→			const num = Number.parseInt(answer.trim(), 10);
    18→			if (Number.isNaN(num) || num < 1 || num > max) {
    19→				resolve(1);
    20→			} else {
    21→				resolve(num);
    22→			}
    23→		});
    24→	});
    25→}
    26→
    27→async function runEnable(): Promise<void> {
    28→	const write = (msg: string) => process.stdout.write(`${msg}\n`);
    29→	const writeError = (msg: string) => process.stderr.write(`${msg}\n`);
    30→
    31→	const credentials = loadCredentials();
    32→	if (!credentials) {
    33→		writeError("Error: Not authenticated. Run `rudel login` first.");
    34→		process.exitCode = 1;
    35→		return;
    36→	}
    37→
    38→	// Fetch user's organizations
    39→	const client = createApiClient(credentials);
    40→	let orgs: { id: string; name: string; slug: string }[];
    41→	try {
    42→		orgs = await client.listMyOrganizations();
    43→	} catch {
    44→		writeError("Error: Failed to fetch organizations. Check your connection.");
    45→		process.exitCode = 1;
    46→		return;
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	if (orgs.length === 0) {
    50→		writeError(
    51→			"Error: No organizations found. Create one at app.rudel.ai first.",
    52→		);
    53→		process.exitCode = 1;
    54→		return;
    55→	}
    56→
    57→	// Check if already configured for this project
    58→	const cwd = process.cwd();
    59→	const existingOrgId = await getProjectOrgId(cwd);
    60→	const existingOrg = existingOrgId
    61→		? orgs.find((o) => o.id === existingOrgId)
    62→		: undefined;
    63→
    64→	let selectedOrgId: string;
    65→
    66→	const [firstOrg] = orgs;
    67→	if (orgs.length === 1 && firstOrg) {
    68→		selectedOrgId = firstOrg.id;
    69→		write(`Using organization: ${firstOrg.name}`);
    70→	} else if (existingOrg) {
    71→		write(`Currently configured for: ${existingOrg.name}`);
    72→		selectedOrgId = existingOrg.id;
    73→	} else {
    74→		write("Select an organization for this repository:");
    75→		for (const [i, org] of orgs.entries()) {
    76→			write(`  ${i + 1}. ${org.name} (${org.slug})`);
    77→		}
    78→		const choice = await promptChoice(
    79→			`Choice [1-${orgs.length}]: `,
    80→			orgs.length,
    81→		);
    82→		const selected = orgs[choice - 1] ?? orgs[0];
    83→		if (!selected) return;
    84→		selectedOrgId = selected.id;
    85→		write(`Selected: ${selected.name}`);
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	await setProjectOrgId(cwd, selectedOrgId);
    89→
    90→	if (isHookEnabled()) {
    91→		write("Auto-upload hook is already enabled. Organization updated.");
    92→		return;
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	addHook();
    96→	write(`Auto-upload hook enabled in ${getClaudeSettingsPath()}`);
    97→}
    98→
    99→export const enableCommand = buildCommand({
   100→	loader: async () => ({ default: runEnable }),
   101→	parameters: {},
   102→	docs: {
   103→		brief: "Enable automatic session upload via Claude Code hook",
   104→	},
   105→});
   106→"}]},"uuid":"5d208d18-9b52-4412-917b-71efa13081e4","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:43.897Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"702afad9-e0f5-44c4-9530-c0ebc82ea4ad"}

{"parentUuid":"f5b450c4-fd97-45ca-8d10-484c168b5e15","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FuFHnoLhgJ9H6yZL6fuMf8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { execSync } from "node:child_process";
     2→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from "node:fs";
     3→import { dirname, join, resolve } from "node:path";
     4→
     5→const HOOK_COMMAND = "rudel hooks claude session-end";
     6→
     7→interface HookEntry {
     8→	type: string;
     9→	command: string;
    10→	async?: boolean;
    11→}
    12→
    13→interface HookMatcher {
    14→	matcher: string;
    15→	hooks: HookEntry[];
    16→}
    17→
    18→interface ClaudeSettings {
    19→	hooks?: {
    20→		SessionEnd?: HookMatcher[];
    21→		[key: string]: unknown;

    23→	[key: string]: unknown;

    25→
    26→function findClaudeDir(): string {
    27→	let dir = resolve(process.cwd());
    28→
    29→	// Walk up looking for an existing .claude directory
    30→	while (dir !== dirname(dir)) {
    31→		const candidate = join(dir, ".claude");
    32→		if (existsSync(candidate)) {
    33→			return candidate;
    34→		}
    35→		dir = dirname(dir);
    36→	}
    37→
    38→	// Fall back to git repo root
    39→	try {
    40→		const gitRoot = execSync("git rev-parse --show-toplevel", {
    41→			encoding: "utf-8",
    42→			stdio: ["pipe", "pipe", "pipe"],
    43→		}).trim();
    44→		return join(gitRoot, ".claude");
    45→	} catch {
    46→		// Not in a git repo — fall back to cwd
    47→		return join(resolve(process.cwd()), ".claude");
    48→	}
    49→}
    50→
    51→export function getClaudeSettingsPath(): string {
    52→	return join(findClaudeDir(), "settings.json");
    53→}
    54→
    55→export function readClaudeSettings(): ClaudeSettings {
    56→	const path = getClaudeSettingsPath();
    57→	if (!existsSync(path)) return {};

    13→function getConfigDir(): string {

    39→
    40→async function getProjectKey(cwd: string): Promise<string> {
    41→	// Prefer git remote URL (portable across clones)
    42→	try {

    44→		if (result.exitCode === 0) {
    45→			const url = result.text().trim();
    46→			// Normalize: strip .git suffix and protocol
    47→			return url
    48→				.replace(/^(https?:\/\/|git@|ssh:\/\/)/, "")
    49→				.replace(/:/, "/")
    50→				.replace(/\.git$/, "");
    51→		}

    54→	}
    55→
    56→	// Fall back to absolute path
    57→	try {
    58→		const result = await $`git -C ${cwd} rev-parse --show-toplevel`.quiet();

    60→			return result.text().trim();
    61→		}
    62→	} catch {
    63→		// Not a git repo
    64→	}

    66→	return cwd;
    67→}

{"parentUuid":"5a324320-9352-4505-b08d-93e3993da549","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→export interface DataPoint {
     2→	date: string;
     3→	value: number;
     4→}
     5→
     6→export function calculateRollingAverage(
     7→	data: number[],
     8→	window: number,
     9→): (number | null)[] {
    10→	if (!data || data.length === 0) return [];
    11→
    12→	const result: (number | null)[] = [];
    13→
    14→	for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) {
    15→		if (i < window - 1) {
    16→			result.push(null);
    17→		} else {
    18→			let sum = 0;
    19→			for (let j = i - window + 1; j <= i; j++) {
    20→				sum += data[j];
    21→			}
    22→			result.push(sum / window);
    23→		}
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	return result;
    27→}
    28→
    29→export function calculateWeekOverWeek(
    30→	currentWeekData: number[],
    31→	previousWeekData: number[],
    32→): number {
    33→	if (!currentWeekData.length || !previousWeekData.length) return 0;
    34→
    35→	const currentSum = currentWeekData.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0);
    36→	const previousSum = previousWeekData.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0);
    37→
    38→	if (previousSum === 0) return currentSum > 0 ? 100 : 0;
    39→
    40→	return ((currentSum - previousSum) / previousSum) * 100;
    41→}
    42→
    43→export function detectAnomalies(
    44→	data: number[],
    45→	threshold: number = 50,
    46→): boolean[] {
    47→	if (!data || data.length < 2) return data.map(() => false);
    48→
    49→	const result: boolean[] = [false];
    50→
    51→	for (let i = 1; i < data.length; i++) {
    52→		const current = data[i];
    53→		const previous = data[i - 1];
    54→
    55→		if (previous === 0) {
    56→			result.push(current > 0);
    57→			continue;
    58→		}
    59→
    60→		const percentChange = Math.abs(((current - previous) / previous) * 100);
    61→		result.push(percentChange > threshold);
    62→	}
    63→
    64→	return result;
    65→}
    66→
    67→export function getTrendDirection(data: number[]): "up" | "down" | "stable" {
    68→	if (!data || data.length < 2) return "stable";
    69→
    70→	const firstHalf = data.slice(0, Math.floor(data.length / 2));
    71→	const lastHalf = data.slice(Math.floor(data.length / 2));
    72→
    73→	const firstAvg =
    74→		firstHalf.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0) / firstHalf.length;
    75→	const lastAvg = lastHalf.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0) / lastHalf.length;
    76→
    77→	const percentChange = ((lastAvg - firstAvg) / firstAvg) * 100;
    78→
    79→	if (Math.abs(percentChange) < 5) return "stable";
    80→	return percentChange > 0 ? "up" : "down";
    81→}
    82→
    83→export function getTrendIcon(direction: "up" | "down" | "stable"): string {
    84→	switch (direction) {
    85→		case "up":
    86→			return "\u2191";
    87→		case "down":
    88→			return "\u2193";
    89→		case "stable":
    90→			return "\u2192";
    91→	}
    92→}
    93→
    94→export function getTrendColor(
    95→	direction: "up" | "down" | "stable",
    96→	isPositiveGood: boolean = true,
    97→): string {
    98→	if (direction === "stable") return "text-gray-500";
    99→
   100→	const isGood = isPositiveGood ? direction === "up" : direction === "down";
   101→	return isGood ? "text-green-600" : "text-red-600";
   102→}
   103→
   104→export function formatWoWChange(percentage: number): string {
   105→	const sign = percentage >= 0 ? "+" : "";
   106→	return `${sign}${percentage.toFixed(1)}%`;

   110→	mean: number;
   111→	median: number;
   112→	stdDev: number;
   113→	min: number;
   114→	max: number;
   115→} {
   116→	if (!data || data.length === 0) {
   117→		return { mean: 0, median: 0, stdDev: 0, min: 0, max: 0 };
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	const sorted = [...data].sort((a, b) => a - b);
   121→	const mean = data.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val, 0) / data.length;
   122→	const median = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length / 2)];
   123→
   124→	const variance =
   125→		data.reduce((sum, val) => sum + (val - mean) ** 2, 0) / data.length;
   126→	const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);
   127→
   128→	return {
   129→		mean,
   130→		median,
   131→		stdDev,
   132→		min: sorted[0],
   133→		max: sorted[sorted.length - 1],
   134→	};

{"parentUuid":"ce35520c-711b-4f5e-b91d-ed62063da92e","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QH3pRPox1fAQCFC2o8bSRt","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { join } from "node:path";
     2→import { $ } from "bun";
     3→
     4→export interface GitInfo {
     5→	repository?: string;
     6→	branch?: string;
     7→	sha?: string;
     8→}
     9→
    10→/**
    11→ * Extract git metadata for a given project directory.
    12→ */
    13→export async function getGitInfo(cwd: string): Promise<GitInfo> {
    14→	const [repository, branch, sha] = await Promise.all([
    15→		getRepositoryName(cwd),
    16→		getGitBranch(cwd),
    17→		getGitSha(cwd),
    18→	]);
    19→
    20→	return {
    21→		repository: repository ?? undefined,
    22→		branch: branch ?? undefined,
    23→		sha: sha ?? undefined,
    24→	};
    25→}
    26→
    27→async function getRepositoryName(cwd: string): Promise<string | null> {
    28→	try {
    29→		const gitRootResult =
    30→			await $`git -C ${cwd} rev-parse --show-toplevel`.quiet();
    31→		if (gitRootResult.exitCode !== 0) return null;
    32→
    33→		const gitRoot = gitRootResult.text().trim();
    34→
    35→		// Try package.json name first
    36→		const packageJsonPath = join(gitRoot, "package.json");
    37→		const packageFile = Bun.file(packageJsonPath);
    38→		if (await packageFile.exists()) {
    39→			try {
    40→				const packageJson = await packageFile.json();
    41→				if (packageJson.name) return packageJson.name;
    42→			} catch {
    43→				// Invalid JSON, continue to git remote
    44→			}
    45→		}
    46→
    47→		// Fall back to git remote origin URL
    48→		const remoteResult =
    49→			await $`git -C ${gitRoot} remote get-url origin`.quiet();
    50→		if (remoteResult.exitCode === 0) {
    51→			const remoteUrl = remoteResult.text().trim();
    52→			const match = remoteUrl.match(/[/:]([^/]+?)(?:\.git)?$/);
    53→			if (match?.[1]) return match[1];
    54→		}
    55→
    56→		// Fall back to directory name
    57→		const dirName = gitRoot.split("/").pop();
    58→		return dirName ?? null;
    59→	} catch {
    60→		return null;
    61→	}
    62→}
    63→
    64→async function getGitBranch(cwd: string): Promise<string | null> {
    65→	try {
    66→		const result = await $`git -C ${cwd} rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`.quiet();
    67→		if (result.exitCode !== 0) return null;
    68→		return result.text().trim();
    69→	} catch {
    70→		return null;
    71→	}
    72→}
    73→
    74→async function getGitSha(cwd: string): Promise<string | null> {
    75→	try {
    76→		const result = await $`git -C ${cwd} rev-parse HEAD`.quiet();
    77→		if (result.exitCode !== 0) return null;
    78→		return result.text().trim();
    79→	} catch {
    80→		return null;
    81→	}
    82→}
    83→"}]},"uuid":"b4c7943b-aa73-425c-8931-de91c7212304","timestamp":"2026-02-26T02:16:57.229Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ce35520c-711b-4f5e-b91d-ed62063da92e"}

{"parentUuid":"13ceb0b1-b767-4d21-b37f-57676826bdfa","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FVnNUf1VbwoPCm2JTKnteN","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import {

     8→import { join } from "node:path";
     9→
    10→interface Credentials {
    11→	token: string;
    12→	apiBaseUrl: string;
    13→}
    14→
    15→function getConfigDir(): string {
    16→	return (
    17→		process.env.RUDEL_CONFIG_DIR ?? join(process.env.HOME ?? "~", ".rudel")
    18→	);
    19→}
    20→
    21→function getCredentialsPath(): string {
    22→	return join(getConfigDir(), "credentials.json");
    23→}
    24→
    25→export function saveCredentials(token: string, apiBaseUrl: string): void {
    26→	const dir = getConfigDir();
    27→	if (!existsSync(dir)) {
    28→		mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
    29→	}
    30→	const data: Credentials = { token, apiBaseUrl };
    31→	writeFileSync(getCredentialsPath(), JSON.stringify(data, null, 2), {
    32→		mode: 0o600,
    33→	});
    34→}
    35→
    36→export function loadCredentials(): Credentials | null {
    37→	const path = getCredentialsPath();
    38→	if (!existsSync(path)) return null;
    39→	const content = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
    40→	return JSON.parse(content) as Credentials;
    41→}
    42→
    43→export function clearCredentials(): void {
    44→	const path = getCredentialsPath();
    45→	if (existsSync(path)) {
    46→		rmSync(path);
    47→	}
    48→}

    36→const rpcHandler = new RPCHandler(router);

    38→const ALLOWED_ORIGIN = process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGIN ?? "http://localhost:4011";

    40→	import.meta.dir,
    41→	"..",
    42→	process.env.STATIC_DIR ?? "public",
    43→);
    44→
    45→function corsHeaders(origin: string | null): Record<string, string> {
    46→	if (origin !== ALLOWED_ORIGIN) return {};
    47→	return {
    48→		"Access-Control-Allow-Origin": ALLOWED_ORIGIN,
    49→		"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials": "true",

    51→		"Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization",
    52→	};

    54→
    55→const port = process.env.PORT ?? 4010;
    56→
    57→const server = Bun.serve({
    58→	port,
    59→	async fetch(request) {
    60→		const origin = request.headers.get("Origin");
    61→		const cors = corsHeaders(origin);
    62→
    63→		if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {

    65→		}
    66→
    67→		const url = new URL(request.url);
    68→

    70→		if (url.pathname === "/health") {
    71→			return Response.json({ status: "ok", timestamp: Date.now() });
    72→		}
    73→
    74→		if (url.pathname === "/api/cli-token") {
    75→			const session = await auth.api.getSession({

    77→			});
    78→			if (!session) {
    79→				return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not authenticated" }), {
    80→					status: 401,
    81→					headers: { ...cors, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    82→				});
    83→			}
    84→			return new Response(JSON.stringify({ token: session.session.token }), {
    85→				status: 200,
    86→				headers: { ...cors, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    87→			});
    88→		}
    89→
    90→		if (url.pathname.startsWith("/api/auth")) {
    91→			const response = await auth.handler(request);
    92→			for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(cors)) {
    93→				response.headers.set(key, value);
    94→			}
    95→			return response;
    96→		}
    97→
    98→		const { matched, response } = await rpcHandler.handle(request, {
    99→			prefix: "/rpc",

   102→
   103→		if (matched) {

   108→			}
   109→			for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(cors)) {
   110→				response.headers.set(key, value);
   111→			}
   112→			return response;
   113→		}

   120→		}

   122→		// SPA fallback: serve index.html for non-API routes
   123→		const indexFile = Bun.file(join(STATIC_DIR, "index.html"));
   124→		if (await indexFile.exists()) {
   125→			return new Response(indexFile);
   126→		}
   127→
   128→		return new Response("Not found", { status: 404, headers: cors });
   129→	},
   130→});
   131→
   132→async function getContext(request: Request) {
   133→	const session = await auth.api.getSession({
   134→		headers: request.headers,
   135→	});
   136→	return {
   137→		user: session?.user ?? null,
   138→		session: session?.session ?? null,
   139→	};
   140→}

   142→console.log(`API server listening on http://localhost:${server.port}`);

{"parentUuid":"3f488925-d502-4146-a483-2bf2d68efd8a","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/chiang-mai","sessionId":"e3f90308-e16d-45bd-ada5-eb45b8529571","version":"2.1.50","gitBranch":"marc/num-6590-rudel-feedback","agentId":"af2a6ef8fb389c34d","slug":"toasty-exploring-lampson","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FAybLBEB9Kvo9bbm3yr2Ex","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type {

     7→import {

    13→// Pricing constants: input=$3/M tokens, output=$15/M tokens
    14→const INPUT_PRICE_PER_MILLION = 3.0;

    18→// ROI calculation constants

    20→const TOKENS_PER_LOC = 15; // Average tokens per line of code
    21→const LOC_PER_HOUR = 30; // Developer baseline: 30 lines per hour without Claude
